title: Ferroelectricity driven interlayer magnetic phase transitions in van der Waals homobilayers
abstract: We demonstrate an effective mechanism for electrically driven interlayer magnetic phase transitions in multiferroic homobilayers, wherein the switching pathway is governed by ferroelectric-polarization-directed band alignments.

title: Advanced wood–inorganic composites: preparation, properties and perspectives
abstract: A review on the modification methods, types of modifiers, and performance improvement of wood–inorganic composites.

title: Catalytic amounts of sodium-sulfonate–naphthol enable mechanically robust, ultra-transparent, super-fire-resistant and easily recyclable polycarbonate
abstract: This work not only inspires environmentally friendly chemical synthesis strategy to fundamentally address fire hazards, additive risks, and plastic pollution, but also paves the way for sustainable lifecycle management of organic polymer materials.

title: A sustainable approach to energy storage in buildings: the first rechargeable geopolymer-based battery
abstract: This paper introduces the first rechargeable geopolymer-based battery, integrated directly into building materials for sustainable energy storage.

title: Incarcerating bismuth nanoparticles into a thiol-laced metal–organic framework for electro and photocatalysis
abstract: A hard and soft acid and base theory guided “ship-in-a-bottle” strategy to incorporate bismuth nanoparticles inside a thiol-rich MOF unlocking dual catalytic activities.

title: Customizing ZnIn 2 S 4 photocatalysts by regulating sulfur sources for C(sp 3 )–H transformation
abstract: Adjusting sulfur precursors to optimize the structure of ZnIn 2 S 4 enables efficient light-driven activation and selective oxidative coupling of ethylbenzene C(sp 3 )–H bonds for the production of bibenzyl and hydrogen evolution in one system.

title: Framing emission gain layers for perovskite light-emitting diodes using polycaprolactone-silver nanoparticles featuring Förster resonance energy transfer and Purcell effects
abstract: This study presents a novel emission gain layer with silver nanoparticles stabilized by polycaprolactone, enhancing Förster resonance energy transfer, Purcell effects, and PeLED performance, leading to higher luminance, EQE, and emission purity.

title: A co-assembly strategy for the construction of robust, recyclable and multifunctional crosslinked metal–organic polyhedra using aromatic polyamides
abstract: This study constructed crosslinked metal–organic polyhedral through the co-assembly of macro ligands (telechelic aromatic polyamides) and small-molecule ligands, achieving remarkable mechanical properties and multifunctionality.

title: Biological metasurfaces based on tailored Luria Bertani Agar growth medium formulations for photonic applications
abstract: The Luria Bertani agar medium is a formidable scaffold for all-biological metasurfaces. To reduce its scattering nature and improve its optical performance while preserving the cell-viability, we propose two new formulations with reduced nutrients.

title: Enhanced performance in transparent conducting materials at the interface of a wide band gap semiconductor and a correlated metal
abstract: PLD prepared SrNbO 3 thin films on SrTiO 3 show enhanced carrier concentrations and mobilities due to charge transfer at the interface. Their performance surpasses optimised correlated metals and rivals doped semiconductors, even at lower thicknesses.

title: Visible light-initiated rapid self-healing of PDMS elastomers engineered through dual dynamic bonding networks for smart sensors
abstract: Multi-band photo-responsive rapid self-healing polydimethylsiloxane elastomers crosslinked through dynamic ditelluride bonds and hydrogen-bonding networks.

title: In situ construction of multifunctional femtosecond laser-induced graphene on arbitrary substrates
abstract: In situ construction of multifunctional femtosecond laser-induced graphene (FsLIG) on arbitrary substrates is achieved by a one-step femtosecond laser direct writing process.

title: 2D MoS 2 /Cu 2 O on 3D mesoporous silica as visible-NIR nanophotocatalysts for environmental and biomedical applications
abstract: A novel photocatalytic platform bridging environmental remediation and biomedical applications, degrading pollutants and generating ROS for photodynamic cancer therapy under low-intensity NIR light.

title: Bacterial cellulose-based functional yarns: from design to applications
abstract: This review highlights recent advances in bacterial cellulose-derived functional yarns, covering applications in reinforcement materials, wearable technology, energy storage, and discolored textiles, with existing challenges and future perspectives.

title: Recent advances in metal–organic frameworks for Li–O 2 batteries: advantages, challenges, and innovative design
abstract: MOFs, as cathodes, photocathodes, and solid-state electrolytes, greatly boost Li–O 2 battery performance by enhancing catalytic efficiency, safety, and energy density—paving the way for advanced, practical energy storage solutions.

title: Magnetoresistance effect of pyridine-capped s -indacene-based conjugated radicals
abstract: A stable radical, 5,7-dimesityl- s -indaceno[1,2- b :7,6- b ′]dipyridine, exhibited a temperature-sensitive interface magnetoresistance phenomenon in its single ferromagnetic device.

title: Nano-enabled dynamically responsive living acellular hydrogels
abstract: Acellular nanocomposite living hydrogels (LivGels) with tunable nonlinear mechanical behavior and self-healing properties were developed using bio-based nanotechnology.

title: On-demand design for elastic metamaterial based on a semi-analytical band gap rapid extraction method
abstract: We propose an elastic metamaterial on-demand design method based on a semi-analytical band gap rapid extraction approach, implemented using the COMSOL-MATLAB co-simulation platform.

title: Hyaluronic acid-based hydrogels modulate neuroinflammation and extracellular matrix remodelling in multiple sclerosis: insights from a primary cortical cell model
abstract: HAS2 mediated hyaluronic acid (HA) synthesis is overexpressed in the cortex after a neuroinflammatory insult creating an extracellular matrix (ECM) scar. This is ameliorated after the treatment with an HA hydrogel through its antiinflammatory effect and ECM remodelling ability.

title: Significant dielectric energy storage enhancement in layered polymer films with heterogeneous carbon/silica nanospheres
abstract: The HCS nanospheres are incorporated into P(VDF-HFP) and subsequently stacked with PEI. The synergistic effect between the high- ε r HCS/P(VDF-HFP) layer and high- E b PEI layer results in a high U d of 10.81 J cm −3 and a high efficiency of 93.8%.

title: Bioinspired leaf vein-architected gold nanowire ecoflexible biosensors for ultrasensitive occlusal force monitoring
abstract: A leaf vein-architected AuNW ecoflexible biosensor, enabling the seamless integration of plant-based substrates with functional gold nanowires for occlusal force monitoring.

title: Experimental and computational insights into CuS–Mg composites for high-performance p-type transparent conducting materials
abstract: Mg incorporation in CuS enhances transparency and conductivity for p-type TCMs. We investigate its optical, electrical, and chemical properties and demonstrate p–n junction diodes, highlighting its potential for semiconductor applications.

title: Processable and controllable all-aqueous gels based on high internal phase water-in-water emulsions
abstract: Processable all-aqueous gels that allow fine-tuning of interconnectivity are created in a single step by photopolymerization of great stable water-in-water high internal phase emulsions (W/W HIPEs).

title: Controllable topological phase transition via ferroelectric–paraelectric switching in a ferromagnetic single-layer M I M II Ge 2 X 6 family
abstract: Recently, the emergence of two-dimensional (2D) multiferroic materials has opened a new perspective for exploring topological states.

title: Dual functional coordination interactions enable fast polysulfide conversion and robust interphase for high-loading lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: DTD inhibits the shuttle effect and modulates the anode interface through coordination interactions, resulting in fast conversion kinetics and a robust SEI under high-loading conditions.

title: Soft, breathable, and recyclable MXene fabrics for wearable electrophysiological recordings
abstract: Recyclable, breathable MXene/gelatin non-woven fabric electrodes for sustainable, reliable, and stable electrophysiological recordings.

title: Textile-integrated multilayer liquid metal soft circuits for multienvironment wearable electronics
abstract: This work presents liquid metal soft circuits combining stretchability, textile integration, water resistance, and multilayer architectures with rigid components, enabling next-generation wearables like an immersive bird's eye view sensing system.

title: S-La 2 Mo 2 O 9 solid solution: a sulfur cathode with a non-shaped matrix enables a better lithium–sulfur battery
abstract: La 2 Mo 2 O 9 is highly dispersed in sulfur to form a solid solution for lithium–sulfur batteries, in which the La 2 Mo 2 O 9 provides high catalytic ability for polysulfide conversion to deliver a high discharge capacity and satisfactory cycle stability.

title: Recent advances in metal single-atom catalysts for ammonia electrosynthesis
abstract: This review provides insight into the development of advanced metal single-atom catalysts for ammonia electrosynthesis.

title: Precise manipulation of iron spin states in single-atom catalytic membranes for singlet oxygen selective production
abstract: Heterogeneous single-atom catalysts are attracting substantial attention for selectively generating singlet oxygen ( 1 O 2 ).

title: A self-powered HgTe quantum dots/PBDB-T:Y6 bipolar broadband photodetector for logic gates
abstract: A self-powered bipolar photodetector based on HgTe quantum dots/PBDB-T:Y6 with a broadband bipolar photoresponse and fast response speed has been shown to achieve five logic gate functions and dual-channel encrypted optical communication.

title: A data-driven approach to interfacial polymerization exploiting machine learning for predicting thin-film composite membrane formation
abstract: A divide & conquer approach and large datasets allowed us to build machine learning models to predict free-standing thin-film formation for membranes.

title: A polymer-free, tumor-microenvironment responsive sol–gel platform for spatiotemporal STING activation and self-amplifying immunotherapy
abstract: iRGD-based hydrogel scaffolds offer versatile platforms for chemoimmunotherapy including tumor cell penetration, STING pathway activation and self-luminescence.

title: Overcoming ACQ: DPA-modified TICT-type AIEgens for high-resolution and multilevel latent fingerprint visualization
abstract: Herein, quinazolinone-based TICT-type AIEgens were developed, converting ACQ to AIE. They exhibit strong blue emission, long phosphorescence lifetimes, and lipid-activated response, enabling high-resolution multilevel fingerprint imaging.

title: Ligand-orchestrated transition metal aerogels: a tailored sulfur coordination strategy for superior oxygen evolution catalysis
abstract: OER activity was significantly enhanced by dual-ligand coordination around Ni, which lowered the energy barrier and stabilized the AEM pathway through multi-metal and electronic engineering.

title: Flexible, self-healing and portable supramolecular visualization smart sensors for monitoring and quantifying structural damage
abstract: A novel supramolecular damage sensor featuring unique ionic recognition, robust failure visualization and excellent environmental adaptability is constructed for monitoring and quantifying structural health.

title: Correction: Regulating Zn 2+ /H + selectivity through functional group design of separators for long-lifespan aqueous zinc batteries
abstract: Correction for ‘Regulating Zn 2+ /H + selectivity through functional group design of separators for long-lifespan aqueous zinc batteries’ by Jiaxian Zheng et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d5mh00358j.

title: Stabilizing molecular catalysts on metal oxide surfaces using molecular layer deposition for efficient water oxidation
abstract: We used molecular layer deposition technology to deposit polyimide to stabilize RuCat for water oxidation, and the stability was significantly improved.

title: Experiment-in-loop interactive optimization of polymer composites for “5G-and-beyond” communication technologies
abstract: The fabrication of high-performance polymer composites for “5G-and-beyond” communication technologies was achieved through experiment-in-loop optimization facilitated by ARD kernel-equipped Bayesian optimization.

title: Two-dimensional multifunctional metal–organic frameworks with large in-plane negative Poisson ratios and photocatalytic water splitting properties
abstract: Two-dimensional auxetic materials designed based on metal–organic frameworks.

title: Rapid 3D printing of unlayered, tough epoxy–alcohol resins with late gel points via dual-color curing technology
abstract: Herein, printing a wide-meshed methacrylate matrix-assisted epoxide network utilizing alcohol chain transfer in a semi-orthogonal curing leads to tough interpenetrating networks much faster, at lower intensities and without layer artefacts.

title: Advances in mechanically active materials for soft wearable electronics
abstract: This article reviews advances in mechanically active materials for wearable electronics. The content covers mechanisms of piezoelectric, magnetic and elastomeric materials, as well as applications in multimodal sensing, haptic feedback and others.

title: Toughening hydrogels with small molecules: tiny matter, big impact
abstract: This review highlights the pivotal role of diverse small molecules in engineering hydrogel network structures and enhancing mechanical toughness. Emerging applications of small-molecule-toughened hydrogels across advanced fields are also explored.

title: 4D printing of carbon-fiber-reinforced liquid crystal elastomers for self-deployable solar panels
abstract: This work reports the design and synthesis of short-carbon-fiber-reinforced liquid crystal elastomer (SCF-LCE) via 4D printing. The integration of passive layers onto the SCF-LCE enables programmable deformations for self-deployable solar panels.

title: Emerging fiber-based neural interfaces with conductive composites
abstract: This review presents conductive composites, fabrication processes, integration strategies, and therapeutic applications in the nervous system of fiber-based neural interfaces.

title: Highly transparent, intrinsically stretchable, photo-patternable, and vacuum-deposited electrodes for wearable sensors and displays
abstract: A highly transparent, intrinsically stretchable, and photo-patternable electrode (T-iSPV) is developed via vacuum-deposited Au bilayers. Stable conductivity under strain enables use in on-skin bioelectronics and stretchable electrochromic displays.

title: High-performance reverse thermoresponsive hydrogel enabled by one-pot PDMS-enriched domain crosslinking
abstract: A reverse thermoresponsive PAM@PDMS hydrogel with ultrastretchability and strong adhesion undergoes rapid, high-contrast optical transitions upon heating, enabling thermal encryption and visual signaling.

title: Accelerated discovery of ultraincompressible, superhard materials via physics-enhanced active learning
abstract: Screening of 2 782 589 crystals in the AFLOW database via physics-enhanced active learning models resulted in a discovery of 632 ultraincompressible candidates (bulk modulus > 400 GPa) and 15 superhard materials (Vickers hardness > 40 GPa).

title: Electrothermally powered synergistic fluorescence-colour/3D-shape changeable polymer gel systems for rewritable and programmable information display
abstract: Bioinspired electrothermally activated polymer gel systems with synergistic fluorescence-colour/3D shape changing properties are developed for rewritable and programmable information displays.

title: Living dynamic polymeric materials
abstract: Many materials exhibit static and functional properties.

title: Recent advances in interface engineering in aluminum matrix composites reinforced by nano-ceramic phases: from atomic structure to mechanical performance
abstract: Overview of interfacial structure types and mechanical properties from different scale perspectives of AMCs.

title: A silk fibroin-based hydrogel desensitizer achieving 660 μm dentin tubule occlusion for dentin hypersensitivity treatment
abstract: A fluid-variable STU hydrogel made from silk fibroin, tannic acid, and urea rapidly seals dentinal tubules over 660 μm deep in 3 min, with excellent biocompatibility, stability, and clinical potential for dentin hypersensitivity treatment.

title: Constructing a biomimetic TiOF 2 @PCN-222-Fe Z-scheme heterojunction using self-assembled L -cysteine for CO 2 visible light photoreduction
abstract: Biomimetic synthesis via MOF-based catalysts to construct a Z-scheme heterojunction provides opportunities for enhancement of visible light photocatalytic CO 2 reduction efficiency.

title: Electron conductive self-assembled hybrid low-molecular weight glycolipid-nanosilver gels
abstract: Controlled reduction of Ag-coordinated self-assembled glycolipid gel drives the formation of Ag nanowires, keeping the gel state, and responsible of electronic conductivity.

title: Introduction to the soft wearable sensors themed collection
abstract: Guest editors Wenlong Cheng, Dae-Hyeong Kim, Nanshu Lu, John Rogers and Alina Rwei introduce this themed collection on soft wearable sensors.

title: Emerging horizons in polymer applications
abstract: Materials Horizons and Nanoscale Horizons Community Board Members Kostas Parkatzidis and Calum Ferguson introduce a collection of recent and impactful articles across both journals, related to emerging horizons in polymer applications.

title: Engineering the nano-bio interface: challenges and opportunities for predicting the surface properties of monolayer-protected nanoparticles
abstract: Nanoparticle surfaces can be coated with a monolayer of functional ligands. We discuss how tuning interactions with biological systems requires careful selection of the nanoparticle core and the physicochemical properties of the ligands.

title: A synergistically designed strain-insensitive conductive hydrogel with humidity-adaptivity supporting sustained functional durability
abstract: A synergistic design enables multifunctional hydrogels with humidity adaptivity and strain-insensitivity, demonstrating application prospects as conductive interconnects in flexible electronics.

title: Unraveling the myths and mysteries of photon avalanching nanoparticles
abstract: This Focus Article dispels 19 common myths and misconceptions about photon avalanching nanoparticles to remove the shroud of mystery surrounding the mechanism, utility, and future prospects of these uniquely nonlinear optical materials.

title: Multiple active site additive-mediated suppression of Sn 2+ oxidation and regulation of crystallization for high-performance Sn–Pb mixed perovskite solar cells
abstract: A multiple active site additive was developed to form strong coordination and abundant hydrogen bonds with perovskites, simultaneously suppressing Sn 2+ oxidation and regulating crystallization in Sn–Pb mixed PSCs.

title: Structural amine-induced interfacial electrical double layers for efficient photocatalytic H 2 evolution
abstract: Surface modification of CdS with positively charged diethylenetriamine molecules and Pt species induces interfacial electrical double layers to optimize charge carrier dynamics, achieving a 26.7-fold enhanced H 2 evolution compared to pristine CdS.

title: Achieving exceptional energy storage performance in PbHfO 3 antiferroelectric ceramics through defect engineering design
abstract: Defect engineering improves breakdown strength, reduces conduction losses and induces localized disorder to achieve an ultra-high energy storage density.

title: Dual organic structure-directing agents in zeolite synthesis: cooperation or competition?
abstract: Cooperation of OSDAs could lead to a certain topology, reduce the synthesis cost, fasten the synthesis, or alter the Al distribution/siting, while the competition of OSDAs may cause phase selectivity issues or intergrowth of different structures.

title: Continuous flow synthesis of metal nanowires: protocols, engineering aspects of scale-up and applications
abstract: This review comprehensively covers the translation from batch to continuous flow synthesis of metal nanowires ( i.e. , silver, copper, gold, and platinum nanowires) and their diverse applications across various sectors.

title: Graphene-based polymer composites in thermal management: materials, structures and applications
abstract: This review highlights advancements in the thermal management of graphene-based polymer composites, emphasizing material selection, preparation, modification, structural design, and innovative applications.

title: Defect-engineered 2D Bi 2 Se 3 -based broadband optoelectronic synapses with ultralow energy consumption for neuromorphic computing
abstract: Ultralow power broadband optoelectronic synapses based on defect engineering of 2D Bi 2 Se 3 for neuromorphic computing are demonstrated.

title: A ladder-type organic molecule with pseudocapacitive properties enabling superior electrochemical desalination
abstract: A pseudo-capacitive organic material with multiple active sites and rigid skeleton structure is proposed, which can be applied in electrochemical desalination system with efficient, fast and durable electroadsorption performance.

title: High-temperature negative differential resistance in tungsten diselenide multilayers without heterojunctions
abstract: High-temperature WSe2 NDR transistors enabled by self-heating, offering simplified integration and high gain for future logic and neuromorphic systems.

title: A self-sustained moist-electric generator with enhanced energy density and longevity through a bilayer approach
abstract: A self-sustained MEG with a dual-gradient (relative humidity gradient and ion density gradient) double-layer aero-hydrogel structure, which enables long-term power generation and ultra-high energy density.

title: Detection of β-transition in polyesters via clusteroluminescence
abstract: An investigation into the conformational effects of polyester on its clusteroluminescence (CL) reveals a temperature-dependent CL response, enabling precise characterization of polymer chain dynamics, including β-transition and glass transition.

title: Cost-effective poly(3-alkylthiophene)-based organic photovoltaics: advancing solar energy conversion and photodetection technologies
abstract: Insightfully connects low-cost poly(3-alkylthiophene) design with tailored morphology strategies for high-performance organic solar cells and photodetectors.

title: Multiple H-bonds induced mechanically robust vat photopolymerization 3D printing poly(urethane–urea) elastomers
abstract: A multiple H-bonds crosslinking strategy is proposed with a poly(urethane–urea) oligomer to overcome the mismatch between the excellent mechanical properties and the good processability of 3D printable PU elastomers.

title: Twist-angle dependent pseudo-magnetic fields in monolayer CrCl 2 /graphene heterostructures
abstract: The realization of twist-angel dependent pseudo-magnetic fields in epitaxial graphene by building a symmetry-breaking monolayer CrCl 2 /graphene heterostructure.

title: Spatially decoupled fluorinated-ether–ester electrolytes for extreme-condition lithium metal batteries
abstract: A spatially decoupled solvation structure is constructed where ether dominates the inner shell for stable Li + coordination, while fluorinated solvents form an oxidation-resistant outer shield.

title: Ion exchange selectivity governs phase selection in zeolite synthesis
abstract: The cation with the highest selectivity coefficient for liquid aluminosilicate oligomers decides on zeolite phase selection.

title: Ultrafast sonochemical synthesis of imine-linked porous organic cages with high surface area for gas adsorption
abstract: This study presents an ultrafast sonochemical method (<5 minutes, ambient temperature) for synthesizing imine-linked POCs with methanol as a green solvent. This method offering 78% less energy consumption and potential for scalable gas adsorbents.

title: High density lath twins lead to high thermoelectric conversion efficiency in Bi 2 Te 3 modules
abstract: By introducing the active telluride Ag 9 GaTe 6 in p-type Bi 0.4 Sb 1.6 Te 3 , lath twin boundaries are induced as differential scattering centers for carriers and phonons, leading to improved ZT values and high thermoelectric conversion efficiencies.

title: Solution-processed spin organic light-emitting diodes based on antisolvent-treated 2D chiral perovskites with strong spin-dependent carrier transport
abstract: The g CPEL for spin OLEDs based on antisolvent-treated chiral perovskite films can be increased by 1.75 times compared with spin OLEDs based on untreated chiral perovskite films.

title: High-performance triboelectric nanogenerator employing a swing-induced counter-rotating motion mechanism and a dual potential energy storage and release strategy for wave energy harvesting
abstract: A triboelectric nanogenerator, based on swing-induced counter-rotating motion mechanism and a dual potential energy storage and release strategy, is presented to harvest wave energy efficiently.

title: Efficient co-production of ammonia and formic acid from nitrate and polyester via paired electrolysis
abstract: A paired electrolysis system was constructed to synchronously valorize nitrate wastewater and upgrade polyethylene terephthalate, utilizing oxygen-vacancy-rich Co 3 O 4 as the cathode and Cu-doped Ni(OH) 2 as the anode, respectively.

title: Heteroepitaxial growth of highly anisotropic Sb 2 Se 3 films on GaAs
abstract: We prepare quasi-1D films of Sb 2 Se 3 on GaAs by molecular beam epitaxy. The aligned grains and anisotropic bonding hierarchy of the Sb 2 Se 3 unit cell together produce giant birefringence in the near-infrared.

title: Ultra-robust polyurethane/lignin elastomers based on multiscale structural orientation induced by pre-stretching
abstract: Ultra-robust polyurethane/lignin elastomers built on multiscale structural orientation induced by pre-stretching.

title: Hydrophobic deep eutectic solvent-based eutectogels for long-term humidity resistance and multifunctional sensing
abstract: A eutectogel with long-term humidity resistance for 365 d exhibits excellent mechanical, adhesive, self-healing, temperature-tolerant, and antibacterial properties, making it suitable for application in strain, pressure, and temperature sensing.

title: 2D materials-based flash memory device: mechanism, structure, application
abstract: Conceptual overview of 2D materials-based flash memory: architectures, materials, and applications.

title: Large-scale preparation of Sb 3+ -activated hybrid metal halides with efficient tunable emission from visible to near-infrared regions for advanced photonic applications
abstract: We synthesized Sb 3+ -doped hybrid Cd( ii ) halides at room temperature. (C 12 H 28 N) 2 CdCl 4 :Sb 3+ exhibits excitation wavelength-dependent emission characteristics, while (C 12 H 28 N) 2 CdBr 4 :Sb 3+ exhibits near-infrared emission under 450 nm excitation.

title: Development of highly robust polyurethane elastomers possessing self-healing capabilities for flexible sensors
abstract: Traditional flexible electronic sensing materials have fallen short in meeting the diverse application needs and environments of modern times.

title: Recent innovations in interfacial strategies for DLP 3D printing process optimization
abstract: This review summarizes recent interfacial strategies for DLP 3D printing process optimization from the view of interfacial interactions between solid and liquid phases which are influenced by resin formation, curing surfaces and light sources.

title: Prediction of scale-free ferroelectricity in the elemental ferroelectric compound M 2 X 5 with distinctive structural prototype
abstract: An elemental ferroelectric compound with scale-free ferroelectricity is proposed by employing tensile strains and/or elemental substitutions to engineer short-range bonding interactions.

title: Correction: Photocatalytic membranes based on Cu–NH 2 -MIL-125(Ti) protected by poly(vinylidene fluoride) for high and stable hydrogen production
abstract: Correction for ‘Photocatalytic membranes based on Cu–NH 2 -MIL-125(Ti) protected by poly(vinylidene fluoride) for high and stable hydrogen production' by Emilia Gontarek-Castro et al., Mater. Horiz. , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d4mh01397b.

title: Eco-friendly colorful particle boards based on metal–ligand coordination
abstract: An eco-friendly approach is proposed for the coloring of particle boards via metal–ligand coordination. The as-prepared particle boards demonstrate tunable colors, superior mechanical properties, and a through-colored composition.

title: Semiconducting liquid crystalline dispersions with precisely adjustable band gaps and polarized photoluminescence
abstract: Two-dimensional metal halide perovskites were transformed into colloidal lyotropic liquid crystals with precisely adjustable semiconducting bandgaps and linearly polarized photoluminescence.

title: An iodine-driven muscle-mimicking self-resetting bilayer hydrogel actuator
abstract: The I 2 -responsiveness of polyethylene glycol-based hydrogel is demonstrated. A new type of fuel-driven bilayer hydrogel actuator using I 2 as a chemical fuel was successfully fabricated.

title: Surface engineering strategies for particulate photocatalysts toward photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production
abstract: Surface engineering strategies, including surface modifications and surface composites, were classified and illustrated to promote photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production.

title: Weakly coupled relaxor construction in lead-free ferroelectrics with simple composition for superior energy-storage performance
abstract: This work proposes a simple and universal strategy to enhance the energy storage performance of perovskite ferroelectrics through single rare earth ion-doping, while minimizing the component complexity.

title: Advances in anion-intercalated layered double hydroxides for supercapacitors: study of chemical modifications and classifications
abstract: The electrochemical performance of LDH-based hybrid supercapacitors (SCs) with improved energy density (ED), enhanced stability, high porosity, and a large accessible surface area has attracted attention as a promising energy storage device.

title: Developing tough, fatigue-resistant and conductive hydrogels via in situ growth of metal dendrites
abstract: We developed a novel strategy for preparing conductive and tough hydrogels through in situ growth of metal dendrites within the gel. The resulting gel demonstrated outstanding conductivity, fracture toughness and fatigue fracture threshold.

title: Covalent organic framework membranes for lithium extraction: facilitated ion transport strategies to enhance selectivity
abstract: Mechanistic design of COF membranes enables high-selectivity ion transport for lithium extraction via diffusion and electrodialysis.

title: Traceable nanoparticles with mitochondria-targeting pyruvate-Cu induce cuproptosis for enhanced triple-negative breast cancer therapy in a mouse model
abstract: Traceable nanoparticles with mitochondria-targeting pyruvate-Cu induce cuproptosis and reprogram immunosuppressive microenvironment for enhanced triple-negative breast cancer therapy.

title: A medium bandgap dimeric acceptor with a high open-circuit voltage for efficient organic solar cells
abstract: A medium bandgap dimeric acceptor DYO-1 achieved an open-circuit voltage exceeding 1.00 V in binary organic solar cells. Ternary devices with DYO-1 obtained efficiencies of 19.6% and 15.8% for small- and large-area devices, respectively.

title: In situ formed hydrogels for soft bioelectronics
abstract: This review summarizes advances in in situ formed hydrogels for soft bioelectronics, highlighting formation mechanisms, application methods, functions with design considerations, emerging applications, and future perspectives for the field.

title: Enhanced selective gold recovery from e-waste via synergistic hetero-atom controlled quasi-planar benzoxazine-based covalent organic frameworks
abstract: A quasi-planar benzoxazine-core-based 2D COF with hetero-atom control achieves excellent Au uptake performance (3467 mg g −1 , >92% selectivity) in e-waste leachate amid 12 competing metal ions.

title: Mechanical training drives structural remodeling of zwitterionic hydrogels
abstract: A special zwitterionic dual-network (DN) hydrogel can be mechanically trained to reconfigure a uniform single network into an anisotropic structure, thereby achieving modulus increase transitions.

title: Bionic fire-resistant chitosan aerogels with the triple stealth functionality: microwaves, infrared and sound
abstract: BPNS@MOF was fabricated via electrostatic self-assembly and grafted onto chitosan, producing chitosan-derived carbon/BPNS@MOF aerogels. The fire-resistant aerogel shows excellent triple stealth functionality.

title: Low-temperature, low-pressure Zn-ion hybrid supercapacitor in extreme near-space application
abstract: The synergistic strategy of weakly hydrogen-bonded electrolyte and hyacinth-shaped Ti 2 CT x MXene@CC cathode with hierarchical bridge-linked structure towards low-temperature, low-voltage Zn-ion hybrid SCs in extreme near-space application.

title: Correction: Is the single-ion conductor cubic Li 7 La 3 Zr 2 O 12 a binary ionic electrolyte?
abstract: Correction for ‘Is the single-ion conductor cubic Li 7 La 3 Zr 2 O 12 a binary ionic electrolyte?’ by Peng Bai, Mater. Horiz. , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d5mh00069f.

title: Remarkably boosted high-temperature energy storage of a polymer dielectric induced by polymethylsesquioxane microspheres
abstract: The polymethylsesquioxane (PMSQ) microspheres can remarkably enhance the energy storage performance of a polymer dielectric at high temperatures.

title: Stretchable hierarchical metal wire networks for neuromorphic emulation of nociception and anti-nociception
abstract: A dual-functional device combines strain-based nociception with neuromorphic memory. Changes in resistance mimic pain responses, while voltage pulses enable memory-like adaptation, offering new possibilities for smart sensory technologies.

title: Synergistic surface doping of organic crystals with source-gated architectures: ultra-stable, high-mobility and strain-insensitive stretchable transistors
abstract: This study presents a synergistic strategy integrating surface doping and source-gated architecture in organic single crystals. Doped SGTs achieve record-high mobility, uniform performance, enhanced environmental stability, and strain immunity.

title: Photocatalytic membranes based on Cu–NH 2 -MIL-125(Ti) protected by poly(vinylidene fluoride) for high and stable hydrogen production
abstract: An effective method for developing a PVDF polymeric stabilizer tailored for a chemically sensitive NH 2 -MIL-125(Ti) photocatalyst was introduced.

title: Multifunctional UV photodetect-memristors based on area selective fabricated Ga 2 S 3 /graphene/GaN van der Waals heterojunctions
abstract: A Ga 2 S 3 /graphene/GaN based multifunctional photodetect-memristor was fabricated using a simple area selective deposition method. Simulations confirm the working mechanism and application feasibility.

title: A high-resolution 3D radiochromic hydrogel photonic crystal dosimeter for clinical radiotherapy
abstract: A photonic crystal dosimeter whose color changes from red to blue with an increase of absorbed dose is studied. The ability to detect the 3D distribution of the radiation field demonstrates its potential for radiotherapy dose verification.

title: Adhesion and affinity of supramolecular adhesives on fluorinated surfaces
abstract: The balance between cohesion and adhesion is controlled by a binary system in which tetrafluorophthalic acid is the functional core of the adhesion to fluoroplastics via fluorine–fluorine interactions and OH⋯F/OH⋯O hydrogen bonds.

title: Directing CO 2 electroreduction to ethanol via delicate geometrical modification of copper-based alloys
abstract: This paper describes a detailed simulation approach to simulate the redox process on CuZn alloys, bridging traditional calculations with large-scale, multi-step processes and identifying key active sites for ethanol production.

title: Comb-like poly(β-amino ester)-integrated PEO-based self-healing solid electrolytes for fast ion conduction in lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: All-solid-state lithium–sulfur batteries (ASSLSBs) using comb-like poly(β-amino ester)-integrated PEO-based solid polymer electrolyte (PPAE–SPE) offer significant advantages in energy density and cycling stability.

title: Photo-crosslinkable organic materials for flexible and stretchable electronics
abstract: Overview of photo-crosslinkable organic components—insulators, semiconductors, and conductors—and their integration into flexible and stretchable electronics such as physical sensors, OFETs, and OSCs via photo-crosslinking method.

title: Ultrathin and permeable silver nanowires/polyvinyl alcohol epidermal electrode for continuous electrophysiological monitoring
abstract: The ultrathin AgNWs/PVA gel electrode with high stretchability, strong adhesion, and excellent air permeance to ensure stable, high-fidelity and long-term electrophysiological monitoring.

title: Anti-freezing conductive hydrogels with exceptional mechanical properties and stable sensing performance at −30 °C
abstract: Conductive hydrogels with stable sensing performance at −30 °C were prepared by utilizing a unique microstructure: MXene bridging PAM and the agar. These findings present insights for the development of flexible tactile sensors in cold environments.

title: Artificial intelligence-driven revolution in nanozyme design: from serendipity to rational engineering
abstract: AI and ML accelerate nanozyme design, optimizing its catalytic performance for biomedical, industrial, and environmental uses. Overcoming data and interpretability challenges, this data-driven approach enhances design efficiency and precision.

title: Reducing exciton binding energy of antimony-based perovskites by improving the phase purity for efficient solar cells
abstract: Enhancing the phase purity in antimony-based perovskites reduced the exciton binding energy for efficient solar cells.

title: Indoor light energy harvesting perovskite solar cells: from device physics to AI-driven strategies
abstract: This review explores the evolution of indoor perovskite solar cells driven by recent advances in material optimization and machine learning, fostering sustainable indoor energy solutions for interconnected smart technologies.

title: High O 2 tolerant metal-based catalysts for selective H 2 O 2 reduction by constructing an ultra-thin oxide passivation layer
abstract: An ultra-thin SnO 2 layer is modified on noble metal-based catalysts as a passivation layer to enhance H 2 O 2 selectivity.

title: Transdermal therapeutic polymer: in situ synthesis of biocompatible polymer using 5-aminolevulinic acid as a photosensitizer precursor and a polymer initiator
abstract: A novel therapeutic formulation, 5-ALA-containing polymeric mixture (5-AP), is disclosed to deliver 5-ALA to deep melanoma tumor sites transdermally.

title: “Macro to micro” interface engineering-manipulated tin disulfide for stable and highly efficient sodium-ion electrified capture
abstract: ZnS/SnS 2 @SG with a “macro to micro” interface engineering design achieves high performance desalination capacity and cycling stability.

title: Data-driven modelling for electrolyte optimisation in dye-sensitised solar cells and photochromic solar cells
abstract: We have developed a data-driven modelling approach to optimising the electrolyte in photochromic dye-sensitized solar cells and have succeeded in simultaneously optimising their visible transmittance and power conversion efficiency.

title: A silicone adhesive with ultralong room-temperature phosphorescence for direct damage visualization
abstract: A room-temperature phosphorescent adhesive (RTPA) designed via a B–O click reaction enables damage visualization in the dark, creating a novel pathway for visual detection, anti-counterfeiting and information encryption.

title: Elucidating and controlling phase integration factors in Co-free Li-rich layered cathodes for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: This article presents the design of off-stoichiometric compositions to mitigate factors compromising phase integrity in Co-free Li-rich layered cathodes.

title: Nanosheet zeolites: controlled synthesis, characterization, and advanced catalysis applications
abstract: This review not only overviews recent advances in nanosheet zeolite synthesis strategies and catalytic applications, but also analyzes their physicochemical properties and effectiveness in diverse catalytic reactions.

title: Dual-band flexible large-area ultrasonic energy conveying via elastic chiral Landau levels
abstract: Multiband Landau levels unlock more flexible ultrasonic wave manipulations beyond traditional topological metamaterials.

title: MAX phase coatings: synthesis, protective performance, and functional characteristics
abstract: This paper reviews the historical development of MAX-phase materials, fabrication techniques for MAX-phase coatings, their protective performance and functional characteristics, and provides an outlook on future trends of MAX-phase coatings.

title: In situ formation of NaTi 2 (PO 4 ) 3 coating layers to enhance the high-temperature performance of NaNi 1/3 Fe 1/3 Mn 1/3 O 2 cathode materials
abstract: The surface NaTi 2 (PO 4 ) 3 coating design combined with partial Ti-atom doping synergistically suppresses electrode/electrolyte reactions and enhances the structural stability of O3-NaNi 1/3 Fe 1/3 Mn 1/3 O 2 .

title: Oscillations in incident electric field enhances interfacial water evaporation
abstract: Mechanism of water evaporation rate enhancement beyond thermal limit.

title: High-performance chemically recyclable multifunctional polyolefin-like biomass-derived polyester materials
abstract: Novel, fully bio-based, and performance-privileged multifunctional polyolefin-like polyester materials with inherent recyclability, exceptional mechanical and barrier properties and ultra-high machinability.

title: Color and fluorescence switchable 2D and 3D printed hybrid materials
abstract: We introduce a light-driven 3D printing ink that consists of electrostatically stabilized inorganic–organic hybrid nanoparticles, a crosslinking monomer and a photoinitiator, enabling the printing of color- and fluorescence-switchable 3D objects.

title: Stretchable polymeric-gel-based sponge with tunable wettability via segmented network design
abstract: We developed a stretchable polymeric sponge with tunable wettability via copolymerization, enabling permanent, programmable surface properties for robust fluid control in applications like oil absorption and wearable electronics.

title: Construction of organic heterojunctions as metal-free photocatalysts for enhancing water splitting and phenol degradation by regulating charge flow
abstract: Electron–hole separation efficiency and catalytic site activity can be simultaneously enhanced by tuning the charge flow direction.

title: Solid-state organic electrochemical transistors
abstract: This review offers a systematic and practical guide to solid-state OECTs. We explore the different classes of solid electrolytes, key considerations in choosing an appropriate electrolyte, device architectures, applications, and current challenges.

title: Stretchable wrinkle-structured liquid metal sandwich films enable strain-insensitive electromagnetic shielding and Joule heating
abstract: Multifunctional wrinkle-structured LM/Ecoflex sandwich films with excellent stretchability are developed, showing multidirectional strain-insensitive EMI shielding performance and ideal strain-insensitive Joule-heating behavior.

title: Iron-catalyzed laser-induced graphene on cellulose paper for solar-driven interfacial evaporation
abstract: Here, we fabricate a laser-induced graphene/iron oxide cellulose evaporator achieving 91% strength gain and 1.62 kg m −2 h −1 evaporation (99% efficiency), enhanced to 1.82 kg m −2 h −1 via folding under 1 sun, enabling scalable water treatment.

title: Pioneering non-thermal plasma as a defect passivator: a new Frontier in ambient metal halide perovskite synthesis
abstract: Non-thermal plasma provides a new realm, facilitating the synthesis and defect passivation of perovskite nanoparticles.

title: Molecular engineering of donor–acceptor-type conjugated microporous polymers for dual effective photocatalytic production of hydrogen and hydrogen peroxide
abstract: PyPh-DBZS and ANTh-DBZS CMPs, combining PyPh or ANTh with DBZS, exhibit high T d 10 > 590 °C and efficient dual photocatalysis: PyPh-DBZS CMP excels in H 2 evolution (133 241 μmol g −1 h −1 ), while ANTh-DBZS CMP leads in H 2 O 2 production (24.51 mM g −1 ).

title: Carbon-dot growth on nanoconvex carbon wires for outstanding optical properties
abstract: The appearance of carbon dot on the surface of carbon wires demonstrates the ability to overcome the optical weaknesses of carbon wires.

title: Catalytic methane dissociation and its non-oxidative coupling in metal-dispersed molten salt media: an ab initio molecular dynamics investigation
abstract: Methane dissociation and its non-oxidative coupling in metal-dispersed molten salt media.

title: Magnetic polarons reach a hundred thousand Bohr magnetons
abstract: Laser excitation of the magnetic semiconductor EuO results in giant magnetic polarons with record-high magnetic moments exceeding 10 5 Bohr magnetons, prompting applications in optoelectronics.

title: Origin and enhancement of magnetoresistance in antiferromagnetic tunnel junctions: spin channel selection rules
abstract: Schematic diagram of a model for spin polarization of an antiferromagnetic tunnel junction caused by an inclined interface.

title: Chain-length engineered interfacial architecture enables dendrite-free aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Critical network regulation—optimization of a DEX network through molecular weight control for achieving ultra-stable Zn anodes.

title: Integrated artificial neurons from metal halide perovskites
abstract: We report a fully on-chip integrated spiking neuron of halide perovskite. Our neuron could be implemented in hardware neural networks that perform certain computational tasks orders of magnitude more energy-efficiently than conventional computers.

title: Manipulating key intermediates and suppressing the hydrogen evolution reaction via dual roles of Bi for high-efficiency nitrate to ammonia and energy conversion
abstract: Doped Bi adjusts the CoS 2 d-band, which boosts the conversion of key intermediate *NO 2 , inhibiting accumulation of NO 2 − and suppressing the competitive HER, consequently realizing a high-efficiency electrocatalytic NH 3 generation.

title: Solvothermally optimizing Ag 2 Te/Ag 2 S composites with high thermoelectric performance and plasticity
abstract: The Ag 2 Te/Ag 2 S composites were fabricated for the first time using a facile solvothermal method for practical applications, and ZT values of ∼0.42 at 373 K and ∼0.38 at 298 K were achieved, both exceeding those of pure Ag 2 S and Ag 2 Te.

title: Hydrogel-based thermoelectrochemical cells for waste heat recovery under passive cooling conditions
abstract: Hydrogel-based thermoelectrochemical cells sustain large temperature gradients under passive cooling, producing up to 35 mW m −2 and a 3.5 mV K −1 Seebeck response. They are robust across orientations, enabling scalable waste-heat recovery.

title: Ferroelectric small molecule enabled high-performance zinc-ion batteries
abstract: To address the challenges of zinc-anode corrosion, hydrogen evolution reactions and dendrite growth in aqueous zinc-ion batteries, we introduce tetraethylammonium perchlorate as ferroelectric small molecules additives in an aqueous electrolyte.

title: Two-dimensional materials for adaptive functionalities in soft robotics
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive overview of the latest breakthrough in 2D material-decorated devices in soft robotics: working principles, manufacturing approaches, challenges and opportunities.

title: Urea oxidation catalysts: a review on non-metallic enhancements in nickel-based electrocatalysts
abstract: This review systematically summarizes the roles of different anions in nickel-based catalysts for the electro-oxidation of urea.

title: NEMS acceleration transducers based on MoS 2 /graphene heterostructure ribbons with an attached proof mass: a simulation study
abstract: Acceleration transducers based on MoS 2 /graphene heterostructure ribbons with a proof mass were modelled and simulated in triaxial directions.

title: Fine-control of electron trapping for photomultiplication in organic photodetectors by modulating the insulating properties of nonfullerene acceptors
abstract: A strategy to control trapped carrier lifetime in photomultiplication organic photodetectors is explored. Improved acceptor insulation suppresses de-trapping, enabling Schottky barrier thinning and enhanced band bending under illumination.

title: Why does silicon have an indirect band gap?
abstract: A new strategy to trace the chemical bonding origins of band structure explains silicon's unusual low-symmetry conduction band minimum.

title: Dissolvable molecular bridges promoting buried interface modification for high-performance inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: We proposed a dissolvable molecular bridge strategy for buried interface modification in inverted PSCs, simultaneously achieving interfacial contact enhancement and buried interface passivation, thereby improving device efficiency and stability.

title: Submicron particles of Cr-substituted strontium hexaferrite: anomalous X-ray diffraction studies, hard magnetic properties, millimeter-wave absorption, and research prospects
abstract: Cr-substituted hexaferrites achieve high coercivity (13.9 kOe) and NFMR frequency (129 GHz), advancing rare-earth-free magnetic and spintronic materials.

title: A strategy for increasing the breakdown field strength beyond the experimental scaling law in yttria films
abstract: Yttria films with nanocrystallite aggregates synthesized via photo-assisted chemical solution deposition achieved an E bf over 12.7 MV/cm −1 , suggesting a new strategy to enhance dielectric breakdown strength beyond the experimental scaling law.

title: Guided ad infinitum assembly of mixed-metal oxide arrays from a liquid metal
abstract: Self-assembly in nano- to micro-fabrication is a frugal approach to advanced electro-optical devices.

title: Vector substrate design for grain boundary engineering: boosting oxygen evolution reaction performance in LaNiO 3
abstract: Accurate control of diverse LaNiO 3 grain boundaries is achieved through vector substrate engineering, leveraging transferrable freestanding oxide layers to enhance the OER.

title: Coordination bonds as a tool for tuning photoconductance in nanostructured hybrid materials made of molecular antennas and metal nanoparticles
abstract: Coordination bonds between molecular entities and the surface of inorganic nanoparticles are an efficient tool to tune both the structural morphology and the photoelectrical properties of nanostructured hybrid materials.

title: Self-generating electricity system driven by aqueous humor flow and trabecular meshwork contraction motion activated BCKa for glaucoma intraocular pressure treatment
abstract: Schematic illustration of the role of piezoresponse nanomaterial (MXene-RGD)-based targeting of BKCa on the homeostatic regulation of intraocular pressure in glaucomatous trabecular meshwork cells.

title: Thermogravimetry in a gradient magnetic field as an efficient quasi in situ method for studying magnetic phase formation: optimizing ε-Fe 2 O 3 synthesis
abstract: Magnetic thermogravimetry enables quasi in situ quantification of ε-, γ-, and α-Fe 2 O 3 nanophases, advancing magnetic phase analysis and synthesis control.

title: Reversible electromechanical manipulation of domain wall in trilayer graphene via ferroelectric sliding
abstract: AFM-tip-driven ferroelectric sliding reversibly manipulates domain-wall solitons in trilayer graphene. Ultralow electromechanical inputs (±2 V, 30–50 nN) enable reliable control of 1D junctions with >90% self-recovery, bypassing high-energy methods.

title: Gate-controllable quadri-layertronics in a 2D multiferroic antiferromagnet
abstract: We introduce quadri-layertronics, wherein the layer Hall effect is engineered into a quaternary mode by manipulating interlayer dipoles via gate voltage in antiferromagnetic multiferroic quadrilayers, enabling selective Hall effect control.

title: Thermally conductive under-liquid adhesives via the synergistic effect of intrinsic interfacial toughness and mechanical dissipation
abstract: An adhesive shows excellent adhesion due to covalent bonds that prevent propagation, while hydrogen bonds undergo rupture and reconstruction and induce crack blunting, synergistically improving intrinsic interfacial toughness and mechanical dissipation.

title: Pushing the boundaries of dielectric permittivity in polysiloxanes: polar dipole modifications enable amorphous pyroelectric polymers
abstract: A polysiloxane containing nitroaniline has a permittivity of 35 at 40 °C. Its TiO 2 composite has two T g s for the polymer in bulk and interface. Poling of this composite leads to a material with a pyroelectric coefficient of 3.4 μC m −2 K −1 at 30 °C.

title: Smart coacervate catalysis: robotic optimization of Knoevenagel reaction networks
abstract: A low-cost 3D-printed robotic platform coupled with computer vision tools provides full automation of the Knoevenagel reaction, ranging from mixing of reagents to kinetic spectrophotometric monitoring.

title: Highly-efficient and scalable TrioN (3N0C) synaptic cell for analog process-in-memory
abstract: TrioN achieves ultra-compact, energy-efficient, and high-speed operation by eliminating capacitors, leveraging only IGZO NMOS transistors for perfect symmetry and scalability.

title: Physics-informed neural networks for programmable origami metamaterials with controlled deployment
abstract: Physics-informed neural networks program full energy landscapes of origami metamaterials directly from governing equations, enabling data-free inverse design and controlled sequential deployment.

title: Simulation of perovskite thin layer crystallization with varying evaporation rates
abstract: Advanced phase field simulations of solution-based perovskite film formation are used to obtain information on film creation that is experimentally challenging to access. This includes the explanation, of why perovskite films should dry quickly.

title: Smart functional binders empowering lithium–sulfur cathodes with enhanced atmospheric stability and catalytic kinetics
abstract: A multifunctional β-PVDF–CoPc binder was engineered with (i) robust polysulfide chemisorption coupled with enhanced sulfur redox kinetics, (ii) exceptional volumetric accommodation, and (iii) enhanced hydrophobicity for air stability.

title: MnO 2 -coated necklace-like CoFe@carbon nanofiber composites for superior electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: Designing heterogeneous interfaces to assemble carbon fibers into necklace-like MnO 2 nanosheet coated CoFe/carbon nanofiber composites for excellent electromagnetic wave absorption performance.

title: Application of carbon-based nanomaterials in Alzheimer's disease
abstract: This review elucidates the use of carbon-based nanomaterials of varying dimensions in the diagnosis and therapy of Alzheimer's disease, grounded on several pathogenic theories of AD, while also outlining present problems and future potential.

title: Medium spin Fe III regulating the peroxide selectivity in the heterogeneous oxygen reduction reaction of spin-polarized Fe-TAML complexes
abstract: A series of Fe-TAML complexes have been utilized to demonstrate the spin effect of Fe III on oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) selectivity, revealing that a higher degree of spin polarization in Fe III favors peroxide (OOH − ) production.

title: Precise regulation of hydrogen bond networks for rapid ion transport in PEO-based composite solid electrolytes
abstract: A controllable hydrogen-bonded interfacial layer is reported by incorporating [3-(2-aminoethylamino)-propyl]trimethoxysilane-modified LLZO nanofibers into the PEO matrix for high-performance composite solid electrolytes.

title: Recent advances in lead-free carbon supported perovskites based on Z-scheme and S-scheme heterojunctions for photocatalytic energy conversion
abstract: Photocatalysis has emerged as a crucial technology for utilizing solar energy to combat global warming and energy shortages.

title: Penguin feather-inspired flexible aerogel composite films featuring ultra-low thermal conductivity and dielectric constant
abstract: Inspired by the penguin feathers, PMIA aerogels exhibit remarkable advantages in serving as thermal insulation and wave-transparent materials.

title: Fully hardware-oriented physical reservoir computing using 3D vertical resistive switching memory with different bottom electrodes
abstract: We propose fully integrated reservoir computing system using 3D-stacked VRRAM with different bottom electrodes. The volatile layer acts as a reservoir, and the nonvolatile layer as a readout synapse for high-density neuromorphic systems.

title: Advanced room-temperature cured encapsulant film for crystalline silicon solar modules: enhancing efficiency with luminescent down-shifting, flame retardancy, and UV resistance
abstract: A multifunctional carbon quantum dot was developed using ammonium polyphosphate and m -phenylenediamine. A downshifted luminescent adhesive film for silicon solar cells was fabricated through a simple, eco-friendly room-temperature curing process.

title: Micromechanical finite element modeling of crystalline lipid-based materials: monoglyceride-based oleogels and their composites
abstract: Digital twins based on true 3D microstructures predict the bulk mechanical response of lipid-based materials and composites made thereof.

title: Correction: Switchable nanoparticle for programmed gene-chem delivery with enhanced neuronal recovery and CT imaging for neurodegenerative disease treatment
abstract: Correction for ‘Switchable nanoparticle for programmed gene-chem delivery with enhanced neuronal recovery and CT imaging for neurodegenerative disease treatment’ by Linying Liu et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2019, 6 , 1923–1929, https://doi.org/10.1039/C9MH00482C.

title: Tuning charge density waves and magnetic switching in carbon nanowires encased in boron nitride nanotubes
abstract: The emergence of exotic charge density waves (CDW) alongside ferrimagnetic materials opens exciting new possibilities for quantum switching, particularly in field-tuning CDW electronics.

title: In situ partial vulcanization synthesis of a conductive and highly catalytic bimetal organic framework for sensitive electrochemical cancer-specific neoantigen detection
abstract: Ultrasensitive electrochemical determination of cancer-specific KRAS G12D neoantigen is achieved via a highly catalytic bimetallic sulfide nanoparticle-decorated conductive MOF nanocomposite and a neoantigen derivatization strategy.

title: Mapping the structure-function landscape of semiconducting polymers
abstract: High-throughput analysis of 100+ semiconducting polymers reveals key structure–property links, with planarity L pp , coupling β , and dipole moment μ as top charge transport predictors. ML accelerates discovery by using these critical features.

title: Recent development review of Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene-based microsupercapacitors: a multi-dimensional analysis spanning from underlying mechanisms to integrated applications
abstract: This review presents the latest advancements in the mechanisms and fabrication techniques of Ti 3 C 2 T x -based MSCs, emphasizes their applications across various components of MSCs, and provides a summary of integrated examples of MSC-powered systems.

title: Recent progress of gel-based materials in energy storage and conversion
abstract: Gel materials are ideal electrode candidates for energy applications, featuring unique 3D networks, tunable properties, and remarkable stability.

title: Human breast milk as a bioactive material platform enabling scarless skin regeneration via macrophage reprogramming and myofibroblast heterogeneity
abstract: Human breast milk as a bioactive material platform promotes scarless skin regeneration via macrophage reprogramming and myofibroblast heterogeneity.

title: Two birds with one stone: natural polyphenols boosted periodontitis treatment of chlorhexidine via reducing toxicity and regulating microenvironments
abstract: Typical natural polyphenol was combined with chlorhexidine to form nanoparticles, achieving the “two birds with one stone” of reducing toxicity and regulating microenvironment, resulting in superior periodontitis treatment outcomes.

title: Synthetic nanointerfacial bioengineering of Ti implants: on-demand regulation of implant–bone interactions for enhancing osseointegration
abstract: Nanointerfacial bioengineering of titanium-based implants with synthetic technologies could improve their osseointegration, showing promise to enhance interfacial osteogenesis, preventing peri-implant infection and overcome osteodegenerative stress.

title: Self-powered wearable sensing devices for digital health
abstract: This review focuses on self-powered wearable sensing devices that utilize human energy, introducing their power supply mechanism and efficiency improvement methods.

title: Ferroelectric nematic and smectic liquid crystals with sub-molecular spatial correlations
abstract: A new class ferroelectric nematic phase forming mesogens with thiophene ring instead of flexible terminal chains are reported. In these compounds the strongest spatial correlations correspond to one third instead of the full molecular length.

title: Environmentally tolerant multifunctional eutectogel for highly sensitive wearable sensors
abstract: Environmentally tolerant multifunctional eutectogel crosslinked by interactions and electrostatic attractions is applied for wearable sensors with high sensitivity.

title: Bioinspired rational design of nanozymes
abstract: This review presents a comprehensive overview of bioinspired rational design of nanozymes, guided by the catalytic mechanisms and structural characteristics of natural enzymes, and enhanced by emerging trend of machine learning assisted strategies.

title: Revealing high coercivity in Nd–Fe–B with gradient rare earth-rich phase sizes
abstract: Gradient rare earth-rich phase (RERP) size distribution is achieved inside Nd–Fe–B magnets via introducing gradient compressive–tensile stresses, enabling directional magnetization reversal, boosting coercivity 11.6% without remanence loss.

title: Continuous monomer supply for highly monodisperse Ag 2 Te colloidal quantum dots for SWIR photodetectors
abstract: A continuous injection strategy was developed to synthesize large-sized, uniform Ag 2 Te CQDs. The resulting SWIR photodetectors showed enhanced photoresponse with a responsivity of 2.0 mA W −1 under 1550 nm illumination.

title: Mixed binary supporting electrolyte approach for enhanced synaptic functionality in one-shot integrable electropolymerized synaptic transistors
abstract: A mixed binary supporting electrolyte system was adopted in one-shot integrable electropolymerization for synaptic transistors. The resulting device exhibited improved memory retention, achieving a recognition accuracy of 95.58% on the MNIST data.

title: Fresh insights into structure–function-integrated self-antibacterial Cu-containing Al alloys: giving Al alloys a new function
abstract: A structure–function-integrated concept was used to design and produce antibacterial Al alloys for the first time ever, giving Al alloys a new function and inspiring fresh insights into structure–function-integrated antibacterial Al alloys.

title: Mixed-linker approach employing a flexible monomer for constructing highly crystalline multiple-component covalent organic frameworks
abstract: Mixed-linker approach employing a flexible monomer for constructing highly crystalline multiple-component covalent organic frameworks.

title: Exploration of halogen-free sustainable superhydrophobic materials for surface protection from multi-contaminants in all weather conditions
abstract: A halogen-free superhydrophobic material for self-cleaning applications, utilizing HMDS-modified MCM-41 with enhanced silanization achieved through heteroatom incorporation showcasing exceptional water repellence and least environmental footprint.

title: Host–guest supramolecular approach for the preparation of a 19 F MRI-guided theranostic agent with long T 2 relaxation times and its synergetic performances
abstract: The construction of highly efficient theranostic materials is critical for accurate lesion diagnosis and treatment.

title: A light-driven ultrafast sensor based on biocompatible solvatochromic metal–organic frameworks
abstract: The design of fast, endurant, and biocompatible porous frameworks with solvatochromism, aimed at addressing the multiple visual sensing of chemicals, is demonstrated.

title: Multi-step functionalization of hydrogels through mechano- and photo-responsive linkages
abstract: Mechanochemistry can be used to pattern soft materials with cell adhesion motifs, where force and light trigger chemical reactions, thereby facilitating spatial organization of adherent cells.

title: Emerging strategies for inkjet-printed perovskite quantum dots in high-performance LED displays
abstract: Inkjet printing offers a precise, scalable method for integrating perovskite quantum dots (PQDs) into LEDs. This review covers advances in PQD ink formulation, printing factors, film stability, and future prospects for industrial applications.

title: One-step fabrication of high energy storage polymer films with a wide bandgap and high melting temperature induced by the fluorine effect for high temperature capacitor applications with ultra-high efficiency
abstract: Limiting the conjugate effect and introducing fluorine resolve wide bandgap and heat resistance conflict, enabling efficient energy storage.

title: Wide-temperature solid polymer electrolytes: Li + coordination structure, ionic transport and interphases
abstract: Solid polymer electrolytes have been considered one of the most promising candidates for solid-state batteries, while the Li coordination structure, interfaces and interphases and related cycling performance still remain challenge.

title: Stretchable and adhesive bilayers for electrical interfacing
abstract: A bilayer interface, featuring a nanoscale SEBS layer, and a SEBS–liquid metal composite layer, is designed to simultaneously achieve the strain-insensitive high conductivity over a broad strain range and strong adhesion with dissimilar modules.

title: Machine-learning accelerated prediction of two-dimensional conventional superconductors
abstract: We perform a large scale search for two-dimensional (2D) superconductors, by using electron–phonon calculations with density-functional perturbation theory combined with machine learning models.

title: Ultralow energy consumption conjugated polymers with perovskite quantum dots via polarity adjustment for photosynaptic transistors
abstract: This study explores Sn-doped perovskite QDs blended with conjugated polymers to modulate charge trapping and decrease energy consumption in photosynaptic transistors, enabling efficient and multifunctional neuromorphic optoelectronic devices.

title: Improvement of optical confinement for high performance perovskite vertical-cavity surface-emitting lasers with the localized surface plasmon resonance effect
abstract: A new strategy for the construction of high-performance perovskite VCSELs by introducing Au NRs. The introduction of Au NRs can improve the optical confinement factor, thereby reducing the laser threshold.

title: Effects of catholyte aging on high-nickel NMC cathodes in sulfide all-solid-state batteries
abstract: Unlocking longevity in solid-state batteries: our study highlights how the sulfide catholyte Li 6 PS 5 Cl outshines halide-based alternatives by mitigating side reactions in NMC811 cathodes, indicating its promise for extending battery lifespan.

title: Intelligent soft wearable bioelectronics for neurological disorders
abstract: This review highlights recent advancements in soft wearable bioelectronics for neurological disorders, focusing on device design strategies and their clinical applications.

title: Pyramid-shaped quantum dot superlattice exhibiting tunable room-temperature coherent emission via oriented attachment
abstract: Oriented attachment drives perovskite superlattices to form mesocrystals, which exhibit coherent optical properties due to long-range ordering and strong dipole interactions, enabling coherent emission at ambient conditions.

title: Hyperelastic superomniphobic surfaces via microprotrusion-induced stress redistribution
abstract: In this work, we report hyperelastic superomniphobic surfaces that have been engineered to retain superomniphobicity, without coating delamination, even at 400% strain and after thousands of stretch–release cycles.

title: Achieving wide-range steep slopes in SnS 2 negative capacitance transistors through an isolated band structure and thermionic emission enhancement via Bi contacts
abstract: SnS 2 with a discrete Sn 5p/5s–S 3p band is integrated with a La:HfO 2 /HfO 2 NC gate stack, achieving sub-60 mV dec −1 switching at 0.4 V. Bi contacts suppress Fermi-level pinning, widening this regime and enabling hysteresis-free, low-power operation.

title: Long-chain crosslinker-induced patterning on an elastic polymer film for robust and reversible information encryption/decryption
abstract: This work reports a long-chain crosslinker-induced surface-patterning method that enables the complex patterns with highly-structural stability at the surface of elastic film for multilevel encryption/decryption.

title: Multifunctional transparent conductive films via Langmuir–Blodgett assembly of large MXene flakes
abstract: Through precisely tuning the surface pressure in the Langmuir–Blodgett (LB) assembly process, multifunctional single-layer Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene films with controlled morphologies are prepared, which are assembled from large Ti 3 C 2 T x flakes (∼52 μm 2 ).

title: Hydrophobic eutectogels with heterostructure for wearable sensing and undersea alarms
abstract: A hydrophobic heteronetwork eutectogel was designed as a wearable strain sensor for realizing undersea communications and smart alarms.

title: Targeted and precise drug delivery using a glutathione-responsive ultra-short peptide-based injectable hydrogel as a breast cancer cure
abstract: An ultra-short peptide-based injectable hydrogel delivers doxorubicin (DOX) in response to the local glutathione (GSH). A single injection reduced the tumor volume by approximately 75% within 18 days without any side effects.

title: Silicon anode modification strategies in solid-state lithium-ion batteries
abstract: This research proposes performance-boosting strategies for silicon anodes in solid-state batteries by leveraging nanostructuring, doping and compositing. Moreover, it surveys the field's perspective and charts future research directions.

title: Chemically robust functionalized covalent organic framework for the highly efficient and selective separation of bromine
abstract: The meticulous adaptation of the selective binding sites into COFs enables the creation of effective adsorbent demonstrate ultrafast sequestration of bromine. The optimized COF adsorbent exhibited selective separation of bromine over iodine.

title: Recent advances in photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production: modification strategies of 2D materials and in situ application of H 2 O 2
abstract: This review summarizes recent advances in the modification strategies of 2D materials for photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production and its in situ applications. Additionally, the existing challenges in this field and potential solutions are discussed.

title: Engineering magnetism in hybrid organic–inorganic metal halide perovskites
abstract: State-of-the-art review of magnetic hybrid organic–inorganic transition metal halide perovskites, focusing on chemical design, the origin and tunability of magnetism, and potential applications in magneto-optics, sensors, and spin filters.

title: From synthesis to properties: expanding the horizons of machine learning in nanomaterials research
abstract: An overview of the application of machine learning in single nanomaterials and nanocomposites is provided. We highlight a closed-loop relationship between the synthesis and properties by machine learning models with optimization and prediction tasks.

title: Liquid ferrofluid synapses for spike-based neuromorphic learning
abstract: Liquid ferrofluid devices emerging as favorable synapses for spiking neuromorphic learning systems.

title: Charge carrier recombination and voltage losses in organic solar cells
abstract: The performance of organic solar cells remains limited by significant voltage losses. To effectively suppress these losses, a fundamental understanding of charge carrier recombination mechanisms in the active layer is essential.

title: A cathode interlayer based on an indandione-terminated quinoidal compound enables 19% efficiency in binary organic solar cells
abstract: We demonstrate, for the first time, a quinoidal cathode interlayer that achieves a power conversion efficiency exceeding 19% in binary organic solar cells.

title: Heterostructural amorphous catalyst decorated membranes with excellent anti-fouling performances in wastewater purification
abstract: The study innovatively combines advanced oxidation technology with underwater superoleophobicity to successfully develop a new type of composite membrane with outstanding anti-fouling performances in complicated wastewater purification.

title: Decoupling of atomic interactions for accurate thermodynamic prediction in FCC alloys
abstract: Decoupling NN/LR interactions enables efficient thermodynamic modeling in FCC superalloys & HEAs/MEAs. Integrated with atomic simulation, it reveals metastable L1 0 superstructures via order–disorder transitions—challenging CSRO interpretations.

title: Tough and circular glass fiber composites via a tailored dynamic boronic ester interface
abstract: The designed boronic ester-based vitrimer forms dynamic covalent bonds with pristine glass fiber surface, eliminating the need for sizing treatments, mitigating delamination issues, and enabling their circular composites.

title: An engineered peptide–polymer conjugate mimics statherin to block dental calculus formation while preserving oral microbiota
abstract: We report a multifunctional peptide–polymer conjugate that can block dental calculus formation while preserving oral microbiota.

title: Dual metal synergistic modulation of boron nitride for high-temperature wave-transparent metamaterials
abstract: The synergistic modulation of BN by dual metallic elements of Ca and Al (0.5Ca–0.5Al–BN) was proposed, which elegantly integrates the advantages of metamaterial-like split ring resonator (SRR) features and h-BN's oxidation resistance enhancement.

title: Spider-silk-inspired self-healing conductive elastomer for joint rehabilitation detection and tactile temperature warning
abstract: Here, a room-temperature self-healing conductive elastomer with bidirectional sensing and thermoelectric properties was synthesized for synergistic biological health monitoring and environmental monitoring to achieve risk prevention in daily life.

title: Synergistic color-changing and conductive photonic cellulose nanocrystal patches for sweat sensing with biodegradability and biocompatibility
abstract: We designed and prepared an optical and electrical dual-signal sweat sensing patch with breathability, biocompatibility, and biodegradability, by using the strong hydrogen-bond interaction between PA and the CNC/PVA network.

title: Integrated iontophoresis and sweat sensing via paper-derived laser-induced graphene soft conductors
abstract: Rationally designed, paper-based laser-induced graphene soft conductors on polyurethane flexible support substrates are fabricated for applications in iontophoretic delivery of cholinergic agent carbachol and electrochemical sweat glucose sensing.

title: Hydrazine-assisted water electrolysis system: performance enhancement and application expansion
abstract: Hydrazine-assisted water electrolysis exhibits various advantages compared with conventional water electrolysis and other hybrid water electrolysis systems in terms of reaction, electrocatalyst design and application expansion.

title: Pressure-triggered polychromatic luminescence in Eu 2+ -activated Ca 8 Zn(SiO 4 ) 4 Cl 2 phosphors for high-sensitivity visual optical manometry
abstract: Pressure-triggered color-tunable emissions and significant spectral red-shift are realized in Eu 2+ -activated Ca 8 Zn(SiO 4 ) 4 Cl 2 phosphors, which make the feasible in remote tri-mode optical manometry.

title: A biomimetic-inspired cross-scale hydrogel evaporator with synergetic light–heat–water–salt management for highly efficient and stable brine evaporation
abstract: Synergistic management of light, heat, water, and salt through a biomimetic gradient structure enables highly efficient and stable brine evaporation.

title: Scalable assembly of micron boron nitride into high-temperature-resistant insulating papers with superior thermal conductivity
abstract: This work reported a universal and scalable assembly strategy to realize kinetically stable dispersion of micron-BN sheets (m-BN) and yield high-temperature-resistant m-BN based insulating papers with superior thermal conductivity.

title: Polar topology in self-assembled PbTiO 3 ferroelectric nano-islands
abstract: This work demonstrates the electrical and mechanical manipulation of topological polar structures in PbTiO 3 nano-islands, which transforms between center-divergent and center-converging types, showing great potential in nonvolatile memory devices.

title: Nanostructure fabrication by area selective deposition: a brief review
abstract: This review introduces area selective deposition and highlights recent contributions toward understanding and developing robust ASD processes.

title: Ultrahigh energy storage density and efficiency in facile dual-layered PVDF/PEI-based nanocomposites via an electrical/thermal synergistic effect
