abstract: Based on the principle of electrical/thermal synergistic enhancement, a series of facile dual-layered polymer-based nanocomposites are designed and prepared.

title: Amorphous nitride semiconductors with highly tunable optical and electronic properties: the benefits of disorder in Ca–Zn–N thin films
abstract: In amorphous Ca–Zn–N, charge carrier concentrations can be tuned across six orders of magnitude, without compromising their high mobilities and while offering tunable band gaps in the range from 1.4 to 2.0 eV, by solely adjusting the Ca to Zn ratio.

title: Unity photoluminescence quantum yield and superior stability achieved in lanthanide-doped lead-free Cs 2 NaInCl 6 double perovskites for highly efficient light emitting diodes
abstract: PLQY of 100% has been achieved through lanthanide doping of the Cs 2 Na 0.9 Ag 0.1 In 0.95 Bi 0.05 Cl 6 double perovskite. By optimizing the dopant concentration, a 5%Yb doping resulted in 100% PLQY, while a 5%Er doping achieved 99.8% PLQY in the same material.

title: Breaking scaling relations in AgAuCuPdPt high-entropy alloy nanoparticles for CO 2 electroreduction via machine learning
abstract: Two-tier ML-DFT screening of millions of adsorption motifs on nanosized HEA particles uncovers scaling-breaking Au–Cu active sites.

title: Recent progress of 3D-printed polymeric gel sensors for flexible electronics
abstract: This review explores 3D-printed solvent-impregnated polymeric gels for advanced flexible sensors, emphasizing their fabrication, versatility, and future potential.

title: Unconventional strong 3p–3d orbital hybridization in sulfur-doped FeSe: for tailored ultrathin electromagnetic wave absorbers
abstract: S doping enhances Fe–S charge transfer, induces strong 3d–3p orbital hybridization, and significantly improves dipole polarization loss. Band gap splitting reduces conductive loss and improves impedance matching with free space.

title: Retaining superior electrochromic performance by effective suppression of ion trapping upon cycling
abstract: Solvation energy plays the key role in determining the cycling stability of electrochromic WO 3 thin films.

title: Harnessing Janus structures: enhanced internal electric fields in C 3 N 5 for improved H 2 photocatalysis
abstract: Janus-C 3 N 5 with a unique homojunction structure enhances electron transfer and light harvesting, achieving 1712.4 μmol h −1 g −1 H 2 evolution.

title: Crystal structure prediction with host-guided inpainting generation and foundation potentials
abstract: This work presents the crystal host-guided generation (CHGGen) framework, which uses a conditional inpainting method on a symmetrized host structure for generating symmetric crystals and a foundation potential for structure optimization.

title: Studies of the mechanically induced reactivity of graphene with water using a 2D-materials strain reactor
abstract: Reactions on distorted graphene membranes show strain dependent reactivity with water as followed by Raman microspectroscopy.

title: Narrowband emission and enhanced stability in top-emitting OLEDs with dual resonant cavities
abstract: Inserting a thin LiF layer as the cathode capping layer with a lower refractive index creates dual resonant cavities, enhancing the narrow spectral emission of red, green, and blue TEOLEDs with FWHM values of 14 nm, 10 nm, and 12 nm, respectively.

title: Biomimetic differently structured and multi-branched hydrogel tubes inspired by bud-growth of plants
abstract: An innovative method to prepare differently structured and multi-branched hydrogel tubes based on sodium alginate/copper ions (Na-Alg/Cu 2+ ) inspired by apical growth and the bud-growth of plants (bud-notching and bud-picking).

title: A general strategy for self-healing elastomers with ultralong room-temperature phosphorescence
abstract: We propose a general strategy to fabricate self-healing phosphorescent elastomers (HPEs) by dynamic B–O bonds, embedding commercial phosphors in silicone rubber for persistent RTP with 2.679 s lifetime, surpassing other reported RTP elastomers.

title: Sieging tumor cells using an amorphous ferric coordination polymer
abstract: “Sieging tumor cells” strategy is proposed based on FeCPs, which efficiently kills tumors while alleviating metastasis. The released SA inhibits the activity of HPA to reinforce the ECM, while DHTA as electron mediator strengthen Fe-mediated CDT.

title: A programmable acoustic metamaterial: achieving untethered ultra-broadband modulation with photoactive structural transition
abstract: This study presents a programmable light-modulated absorber using photo-responsive actuators to achieve untethered ultra-broadband acoustic modulation, addressing the growing need for low-frequency attenuation in varying environments.

title: Durable and highly absorptive ant-nest structured superhydrophobic sponge for efficient de-icing and interfacial evaporation in polar environments
abstract: A novel solar-driven superhydrophobic sponge with high photothermal efficiency enables anti-icing/de-icing and interfacial evaporation under extreme cold, offering a sustainable solution for polar photothermal de-icing and seawater desalination.

title: A biomass-derived multifunctional conductive coating with outstanding electromagnetic shielding and photothermal conversion properties for integrated wearable intelligent textiles and skin bioelectronics
abstract: A sensor integrating intelligent textiles and skin bioelectronics was developed, demonstrating high sensitivity to electrical signals and motion detection. The textile shows excellent photothermal conversion and electromagnetic shielding.

title: Topological regulation in polysilsesquioxanes for achieving super-hard and flexible membranes: insights from molecular simulation
abstract: Precisely controlling the molecular framework significantly enhances the hardness of polysiloxane to a record 1.56 GPa, offering a novel strategy for developing polymer coatings with exceptional hardness and high flexibility.

title: Effect of physicochemical congestion on the catalytic conversion of arylboronic acids to phenols
abstract: Two-dimensional nanosheets of Ni(OH) 2 were assembled into a 2D nanofluidic membrane to alter the free energy of hydroxylation of phenylboronic acids to phenols.

title: Precision-engineered, polymer-lean, digital light processing 3D-printed hydrogels for enhancing solar steam generation and sustainable water treatment
abstract: Innovative digital light processing 3D printing ink specifically designed for hydrogels minimizes precursor content, enhances porosity, and improves water evaporation efficiency in interfacial solar steam generation.

title: Functional antimicrobial peptide-loaded 3D scaffolds for infected bone defect treatment with AI and multidimensional printing
abstract: Infection is the most prevalent complication of fractures, particularly in open fractures, and often leads to severe consequences.

title: Dynamic Mo leaching and vacancy engineering synergize HER and OER kinetics in NiFe-based catalysts for overall water electrolysis
abstract: Dynamic Mo leaching in NiFe-LDH generates oxygen vacancies that drive OER surface reconstruction and optimize the HER microenvironment, enabling durable water electrolysis in AEMWE.

title: Bioinspired and 3D-printed solar evaporators for highly efficient freshwater-electricity co-generation
abstract: A bioinspired water-electricity co-generation system was developed, featuring a polydopamine-coated film and a 3D-printed microchannel support, enabling seamless integration of water evaporation and electricity generation.

title: Machine learning-assisted design of cathode materials for lithium–sulfur batteries derived from a metal–organic framework
abstract: A machine learning (ML)-designed MOF as a carbon precursor for the Li–S battery cathode showed capacity retention closely matching ML predictions.

title: Ultraviolet durable and recyclable radiative cooling covering for efficient building energy saving
abstract: A recyclable polymer-based porous radiative cooling film with excellent ultraviolet durability was developed as a covering of a building.

title: Self-powered composites by bioinspired device-to-material integration
abstract: We report self-powered composites (SPCs) by embedding rigid coin cells in thermoplastics and thermosets. The resultant SPCs are successfully demonstrated as a promising building block for self-powered electrical vehicles and wearable electronics.

title: Rewritable wavelength-selective hydrogel actuators grafted with fluorophores
abstract: A novel fabrication method for fluorophore-grafted hydrogel actuators enables rewritable wavelength-selective actuation via photothermal effects and iterative bleaching of fluorophores.

title: Suppressing intergranular cracking with near-surface layer regulation for electrochemical-thermal stabilization of LiCoO 2
abstract: By regulating Mg²⁺ diffusion in the near-surface lithium layer and performing surface modification, we enhanced the stability of the layered structure, suppressed intergranular cracking, and optimized performance under extreme conditions.

title: Lattice plainification flattens the crystal structure of nickel-rich layered cathodes
abstract: A lattice plainification approach eliminates the rocksalt and disordered phases, and suppresses oxygen, lithium, and transition metal vacancies, as well as Li/Ni mixing, which improves the structure degradation, mechanical failures, and gas release.

title: Substantially enhanced high-temperature capacitive performance in BOPP films via coating with a magnetic inorganic nanolayer
abstract: This study involved depositing a wide-bandgap cobalt ferrite insulating nanolayer onto the surface of BOPP films using magnetron sputtering technology. This composite material exhibits excellent capacitance characteristics at high temperatures.

title: Lanthanide–polyoxometalate-based self-erasing luminescent hydrogels with time-dependent and resilient properties for advanced information encryption
abstract: A self-erasing luminescent hydrogel based on lanthanide–polyoxometalate with time-dependent and resilient properties was developed to improve the level of information security.

title: Self-assembled monolayers for tin perovskite solar cells: challenges and opportunities
abstract: This review explores the potential of self-assembled monolayers (SAMs) as a solution to mitigate the voltage deficits in tin perovskite solar cells (TPSCs).

title: Recent advances in atmospheric water harvesting technology and its development
abstract: This article provides an overview of the advancements in atmospheric water collection driven by solar energy. The roles of materials, surfaces, equipment, and systems in enhancing water collection efficiency are discussed.

title: Construction of an ultrathin multi-functional polymer electrolyte for safe and stable all-solid-state batteries
abstract: An ultrathin, flexible and mechanical robust solid polymer electrolyte based on a rationally designed polymer network is developed.

title: A layer-by-layer assembled superhydrophobic composite aerogel for rapid and high-capacity removal of microplastics from beverages
abstract: A superhydrophobic composite aerogel, AG U6-(OH) 2 @PMSQ, was successfully fabricated using a layer-by-layer in situ growth strategy based on a robust dual-network structure. This material demonstrated an outstanding adsorption capacity for PSM.

title: Flexible silicon for high-performance photovoltaics, photodetectors and bio-interfaced electronics
abstract: This review highlights the advancements in flexible silicon-based photovoltaics, photodetectors, and bio-interfaced electronics, identifying key design principles through control of geometry, morphology, and composition.

title: Fullerene-derivative-induced hilly morphology for high-speed flexible organic electrochemical transistors
abstract: Decoupled and enhanced ionic/electronic transport in blended OECTs achieves fast response via fullerene-derivative-induced hilly morphology.

title: GO-HNT framework-based hydrogels with efficient water evaporation-driven cooling and superior electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: Heat dissipation and electromagnetic wave absorption (EWA) are crucial for mitigating heat accumulation and electromagnetic interference issues in electronics.

title: Multistable metamaterials enabled by arched beams for high-efficiency energy absorption
abstract: Energy-absorbing materials with high absorption efficiency are ideal candidates for impact protection.

title: Application of advanced quantum dots in perovskite solar cells: synthesis, characterization, mechanism, and performance enhancement
abstract: This review focuses on the synthesis of quantum dots, film deposition techniques, and their application across various functional layers in perovskite solar cells, elucidating the mechanisms by which quantum dots enhance photovoltaic performance.

title: Organic dual-channel transistors for reconfigurable signal modulation and anti-counterfeiting
abstract: The designed dual-channel transistor displays unique device characteristics ( I D ∝ V 0G when V D < V G − V T and I D ∝ V 1G when V D ≥ V G − V T ), which enable reconfigurable signal modulation and anti-counterfeiting.

title: Array-level MXene electrode flexible long-term-plasticity synaptic transistors
abstract: Novel array-level MXene-electrode flexible synaptic transistors (MEFST) have been realized with a long-term synaptic plasticity of over 1000 s without requiring a floating gate structure, electrolyte dielectrics, or ferroelectric materials.

title: Reconfigurable double-sided smart textile circuit with liquid metal
abstract: A liquid metal and silver flakes-based smart textile enables double-sided circuit designs without via holes. Pressure-controlled sintering allows dynamic insulation or interlayer connection, supporting reconfigurable wearable electronics.

title: Acoustic metamaterials for remote manipulation of large objects in water
abstract: Underwater acoustic metamaterials are developed to reshape the acoustic radiation force, enabling remote, multi-degree-of-freedom manipulation of large objects in water.

title: Unlocking triple-band persistent luminescence: invisible UVC–UVB and visible red emission through structural modulation of Na 2 SrSi 2 O 6 :Pr 3+ cyclosilicate
abstract: The demonstration of unprecedented triple-band persistent luminescence spanning ultraviolet-C, ultraviolet-B and visible red spectral regions through the engineering of Na 2 SrSi 2 O 6 cyclosilicate matrices.

title: Piezo-to-piezo (P2P) conversion: simultaneous β-phase crystallization and poling of ultrathin, transparent and freestanding homopolymer PVDF films via MHz-order nanoelectromechanical vibration
abstract: An unconventional yet facile low-energy method for uniquely synthesizing neat poly(vinylidene fluoride) (PVDF) films for energy harvesting applications by utilizing high frequency nanoelectromechanical vibration is reported.

title: Ligand effect on In–Ti-oxo nanoclusters for nanolithography
abstract: This study employs a ligand regulation strategy to isolate isomeric In 4 Ti 12 -cores with varying spherical shells, revealing distinct differences in solubility, stability, film-forming ability, and lithography performance.

title: Highly salt-resistant and efficient dynamic Janus absorber based on thermo-responsive hydroxypropyl cellulose
abstract: We present a Janus hydrogel-based absorber featuring a surface modified with thermo-responsive hydroxypropyl cellulose and a hydrogel matrix containing photothermal conversion units, MXene, specifically designed for long-term seawater desalination.

title: Correction: Transforming patterned defects into dynamic poly-regional topographies in liquid crystal oligomers
abstract: Correction for ‘Transforming patterned defects into dynamic poly-regional topographies in liquid crystal oligomers’ by Yuxin You et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2024, 11 , 3178–3186, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4MH00131A.

title: Is the single-ion conductor cubic Li 7 La 3 Zr 2 O 12 a binary ionic electrolyte?
abstract: Garnet-type cubic Li 7 La 3 Zr 2 O 12 (c-LLZO) is a single-ion conductor, but the electrochemically generated charged vacancies make it a binary ionic electrolyte, which explains the current- and thickness-dependent lithium dendrite initiation dynamics.

title: Tuning of anomalous magnetotransport properties in half-Heusler topological semimetal GdPtBi
abstract: The Fermi level in archetypal topological semimetal GdPtBi was shifted using high-energy electron irradiation. This shift resulted in significant changes in both negative longitudinal magnetoresistance and the anomalous Hall effect.

title: Deep learning-enhanced development of innovative antioxidant liposomal drug delivery systems from natural herbs
abstract: Discovery and construction of antioxidant-liposomal platforms from natural herbs based on the BERT model and fully validated in vitro and in vivo .

title: Neuromorphic devices for electronic skin applications
abstract: This paper illustrates future research directions for neuromorphic e-skin devices and their applications.

title: Room-temperature self-healing waterborne glassy polyurethane via a disordered hydrogen bonding network
abstract: We report a waterborne glassy polyurethane with disordered hydrogen bonds that enables room-temperature self-healing, high modulus (1.5 GPa), and yield strength (39.1 MPa), while remaining recyclable in ethanol, offering a sustainable solution.

title: Two-dimensional GaN/Si heterojunctions towards high-performance UV-B photodetectors
abstract: Wafer-scale 2D GaN with a tunable bandgap is realized on a Si substrate via a two-step method consisting of magnetron sputtering and high temperature ammonolysis, providing a resolution for high performance UV-B photodetectors.

title: Acetylene semi-hydrogenation catalyzed by Pd single atoms sandwiched in zeolitic imidazolate frameworks via hydrogen activation and spillover
abstract: Pd single atoms were anchored by zeolitic imidazolate framework to form a Z@Pd@Z sandwich catalyst, over which efficient acetylene semi-hydrogenation could be realized by hydrogen activation and spillover.

title: Is Mg 3 AsN antiperovskite a promising Mg-ion conductor?
abstract: The electronic conductivity of Mg 3 AsN hinders its application in RMBs. Applying an electron-blocking matrix, such as MOFs or polymer, unlocked its potential, facilitating reversible Mg deposition/stripping processes.

title: Recent achievements in noble metal-based oxide electrocatalysts for water splitting
abstract: Noble metal-based oxide electrocatalysts are essential for the development of H 2 production technology by water electrolysis, and this review summarises the recent research progress of noble metal oxides in the field of water electrolysis.

title: Porous polymers: structure, fabrication and application
abstract: This review systematically examines the structural features, fabrication techniques, and applications of porous polymers, as well as their interrelationships, to serve as a guide for researchers.

title: Mass production of robust hydrogel electrolytes for high-performance zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Hydrogel electrolytes prepard by superspreading strategy, which effectively suppress zinc dendrite growth and side reactions, enabling ultra-stable cycling in zinc-ion batteries. This makes zinc batteries highly promising for large-scale applications.

title: An LDH-enhanced porous polyethersulfone membrane doped with alkali for anion exchange membrane fuel cells
abstract: In situ grown LDH in porous PES membranes enhances alkali retention, enabling durable, high-performance AEMFCs with recoverable activity post-degradation.

title: Octuple-state sliding ladder ferroelectrics in bilayer van der Waals GeSe/SnS heterostructures
abstract: Advancing next-generation high-density data storage and post-Moore computing technologies requires the engineering of higher-order multistate ferroelectric transitions.

title: Liquid metal microparticles enabled low-cost, compact, and sensitive humidity sensors for in situ moisture monitoring
abstract: A liquid metal microparticle-enabled humidity sensor (referred to as LM 2 H) is passive, low-cost, highly sensitive, and ultra-compact.

title: Tunable photo-responsive liquid crystal elastomer fibers via disperse dyeing for smart textiles
abstract: A two-step cross-linking and dispersion dyeing strategy was proposed to create three wavelength-selective liquid crystal elastomer fibers, used to produce functional smart textiles via various fabrication techniques.

title: Phosphorylation-assisted cell wall engineering enables ultra-strong, highly ion-conductive bio-membranes for high-power salinity gradient energy harvesting
abstract: All-wood-based nanofluidic membranes with superior ionic conductivity, selectivity, and mechanical strength for salinity-gradient power conversion.

title: Long-life graphite–lithium sulfide full cells enabled through a solvent Co-intercalation-free electrolyte design
abstract: An optimized ether-based electrolyte is designed to suppress solvent co-intercalation and decomposition of graphite anodes, thereby enabling lithium–sulfur batteries with low N/P ratio.

title: Respirator-inspired shielding and catalytic oxidation strategies for smoke-suppression polymers, enhancing fire safety
abstract: Polymeric materials pose potential fire hazards to both humans and property because of their intrinsic flammability and the toxic smoke generated by them upon burning.

title: Crystal reconstructed cubic nickel oxide with energetic reactive interfaces for exceptional electrochromic smart windows
abstract: The reconstruction of cubic phase NiO by phosphorylation extremely improves ion transport efficiency and electrochromic properties.

title: Uniqueness and future perspectives of 2D ferroelectric devices: applications in emerging computing paradigms and hardware security
abstract: This review charts the rise of 2D ferroelectrics and their shift from fundamental physics to devices, enabling energy-efficient in-sensor computing, logic-in-memory, reservoir computing, and hardware security primitives like TRNGs and PUFs.

title: Self-recoverable broadband near infrared mechanoluminescence from BaGa 12 O 19 :Cr 3+ using a multi-site occupation strategy
abstract: Self-recoverable broadband near-infrared mechanoluminescence was obtained in Cr 3+ ion-doped BaGa 12 O 19 by a multi-lattice site occupation strategy, with an intensity of nearly 66.97% after penetrating ~5 mm thick pork tissue.

title: Poly(3-hexylthiophene) as a versatile semiconducting polymer for cutting-edge bioelectronics
abstract: Photo- and electro-active poly(3-hexylthiophene) (P3HT)-based materials serve as efficient and highly biocompatible phototransducers for cutting-edge bioelectronic applications.

title: Supramolecular transparent plastic engineering via covalent-and-supramolecular polymerization
abstract: Inspired by the toughening phenomena in biological systems, a synergistic covalent-and-supramolecular polymerization strategy was applied to construct mechanically strong transparent materials.

title: Muscle-like hydrogels with fast isochoric responses and their applications as soft robots: a minireview
abstract: Various muscle-like hydrogels that emerged in recent years exhibit rapid and isochoric deformations upon stimulations. This minireview summarizes the development of muscle-like hydrogels, their unique mechanisms, and applications as soft robots.

title: Optimizing dielectric, mechanical, and thermal properties of epoxy resin through molecular design for multifunctional performance
abstract: Epoxy resins are widely used as dielectric materials in electrical and electronic systems. This study demonstrates that small amounts of chemical modifiers can significantly enhance epoxy resin performance.

title: Ultrathin WO 3 ·H 2 O nanosheets derived from the Aurivillius phase for high-performance dual-band electrochromic smart windows
abstract: An ultrathin nanosheet structure was obtained via ion etching of Aurivillius Bi 2 WO 6 and liquid-phase exfoliation, endowing a WO 3 ·H 2 O film with comprehensive and remarkable electrochromic properties in the visible-near-infrared wavelength range.

title: Unravelling the aerodynamic enhancement of water harvesting via dynamic liquid bumps
abstract: The in situ deposition of microdroplets on smooth hydrophobic surfaces can enhance total water harvesting via the aerodynamic process, which unravels the previous confusion in fog collection.

title: Advances in conductive filler-integrated hydrogels and derived aerogels: innovative strategies for electromagnetic interference shielding
abstract: Electromagnetic interference has promoted the development of hydrogel shielding materials. This review explores their shielding mechanisms, research progress, challenges and future directions.

title: Alloying multiple halide perovskites on the same sublattice in search of stability and target band gaps
abstract: Single-component halide perovskites (HPs) rarely satisfy all the necessary criteria for optoelectronic applications, such as achieving an optimal band gap while maintaining high chemical and structural stability.

title: High-performance organic thermoelectric materials based on n-type conjugated polymers via chemical isomerization-induced charge transport modulation
abstract: Chemical isomerization of n-type conjugated polymers induces structural ordering and charge transport modulation, enabling enhanced thermoelectric performance through energy-filtering effects.

title: Dynamics of disorder in mixed ionic–electronic transport in cross-linked non-conjugated redox polymers
abstract: The coupled electronic and ionic transport mechanisms in organic mixed ionic–electronic conductors (OMIECs) remain elusive to rationalize.

title: A review on electrode materials of supercapacitors used in wearable bioelectronics and implantable biomedical applications
abstract: Supercapacitors boost wearable/implantable devices with high power & long life. Novel materials aim for better energy density & miniaturization, powering bioelectronics & medical implants with reliable, safe energy.

title: Strategic defect engineering at the buried interface for metal–halide transistors
abstract: A synergistic processing strategy for defect engineering at the buried interface of CuI TFTs is developed. Ambient-dependent processing is optimized to modulate vacancies/defects at the buried interface, achieving superior performance and stability.

title: Electron-deficient B-site engineering in CeNiO 3 for efficient PMS activation and tetracycline degradation
abstract: The electron-deficient Ni active sites in CeNiO 3 were designed by Fe doping strategy, thereby promoting the activation of peroxymonosulfate (PMS).

title: Tri-continuous polymer templates enable scalable fabrication of hierarchical nanoparticle monoliths
abstract: Tri-continuous polymer blend nanocomposites provide a novel templating route to hierarchical monoliths with interconnected porosity, offering scalability and functional versatility.

title: Light-regulated pyro-phototronic effects in a perovskite Cs 2 SnI 6 -reinforced ferroelectric polymer hybrid nanostructure
abstract: The ‘pyro-phototronic effect’ plays a nontrivial role in advancing ferroelectric (FE) devices of light detectors, light-emitting diodes, and other smart technologies.

title: Upcycling of mixed polyolefin wastes to 3D structured carbon Joule heaters for decarbonized hydrogen production
abstract: This work presents an integrated approach to industrial decarbonization by converting mixed polyolefin waste into structured carbon with exceptional Joule heating properties, enabling efficient electrified hydrogen production via NH 3 decomposition.

title: Regulation of metal valence states for enhancing second harmonic generation performance of chiral tin halides
abstract: The oxidation strategy applied to 0D chiral tin halides, which converts Sn( ii ) to Sn( iv ), enhances their nonlinear optical properties by modulating structural symmetry, resulting in a 65-fold increase in SHG response.

title: White light-assisted projection printing of submicron plasmonic nanostructures for advanced nanofabrication
abstract: The white light-based plasmonic printing technique demonstrates a groundbreaking innovation for realizing plasmonic nanostructures, complex patterns, conductive patterns, SERS substrates, and optothermal trapping.

title: 2D van der Waals heterostructure memristors: from band structure regulation to neuromorphic computing applications
abstract: 2D van der Waals heterostructures offer great promise for memristors and neuromorphic computing. This review covers their preparation methods, band structures, switching mechanisms in memristors, and neuromorphic computing applications.

title: Bioinspired self-driven realignment and healing in multilayer triboelectric generators enabled by reversibly actuated polyurethane
abstract: This work proposes an autonomous realignment and self-healing mechanism for multilayer triboelectric generators (TEGs) based on a reversibly actuated polyurethane, consequently achieving good and stable functional recovery after damage.

title: Revealing the reversal of the anomalous hall effect and the exchange bias-like effect in single-phase perpendicularly magnetized NiCo 2 O 4 epitaxial films
abstract: In this work, the NiCo 2 O 4 films exhibit perpendicular magnetic anisotropy and metallic behavior. The relationship between the anomalous Hall effect, exchange bias-like effect, and cation valence states or concentrations is established.

title: A novel design strategy for tetradentate Pt( ii ) complexes using conformation managers to achieve high-efficiency, narrow-emission blue organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: In this study, we developed a molecular design strategy for tetradentate Pt( ii ) complexes to improve the efficiency and color purity of blue phosphorescent organic light-emitting diodes (PhOLEDs).

title: In situ SERS and in situ Raman: deciphering interfacial phenomena and processes
abstract: This review highlights in situ SERS/Raman for dissecting complex reactions via spectral analysis. It covers species transformation and substrate evolution in various systems, and discusses statistical methods and future perspectives.

title: Advances in chiral luminescent liquid crystals (CLLCs): from molecular design to applications
abstract: Research on circularly polarized luminescent (CPL) materials has evolved into a hot research topic because of their potential application prospects in the optoelectronics and chiroptical fields.

title: Enabling accurate modelling of materials for a solid electrolyte interphase in lithium-ion batteries using effective machine learning interatomic potentials
abstract: Overview of the active learning workflow utilized to construct the moment tensor potential for a solid electrolyte interphase.

title: A heterogeneous nanocomposite architecture with contrasting thermal conductivity and hydrophilicity for synergistic solar-thermal storage and evaporation
abstract: A 3D graphene architecture with a hydrophilic gradient hydrogel evaporator and a hydrophobic thermal storage composite achieves an evaporation rate of 3.6 kg m −2 h −1 under sunlight, maintaining 2.7 kg m −2 h −1 for 30 minutes when sunlight dims.

title: Patternable, high-precision, controllable wettability copper layers for 3D resin-based weather-resistant electronics and 3D liquid manipulation
abstract: 3D patterned copper layers with spontaneously formed biomimetic structures and controllable wettability are prepared on different 3D-printed resin parts, which can be used for 3D droplet manipulation or 3D weather resistance electronics.

title: Tough and sustainable solid–solid phase change materials achieved via reversible crosslinking for thermal management
abstract: A dynamic chemical cross-linking method has been proposed to synthesize leakage-proof and tough PCMs for thermal management of modern electronics. The PCMs exhibit excellent shape memory feature, recyclability and self-healing property.

title: Scaled-down ionic liquid-functionalized geopolymer memristors
abstract: Ionic liquid-functionalized geopolymers fabricated at the micron-scale exhibit enhanced memristive retention and biologically relevant synaptic functions, establishing a sustainable platform for neuromorphic hardware integration.

title: Shape-morphing bioelectronic devices
abstract: Working principles, representative deformation modes, manufacturing methodologies, diverse applications, as well as challenges and opportunities of shape-morphing bioelectronic devices.

title: Thermoelectric transport and the role of different scattering processes in the half-Heusler NbFeSb
abstract: Developed and employed a novel method offering ab initio accuracy with 10X–100X computational savings and providing detailed scattering process descriptions for full understanding of transport properties for best performing half-Heusler NbFeSb.

title: Enhancing liquid–vapor phase behavior through multiscale anisotropic wettability gradient in dandelion-inspired nanostructures
abstract: This research developed an approach to engineering multiscale anisotropic wettability gradients through the design of dandelion-inspired nanostructures, enabling both horizontal and vertical biphilicity.

title: Highly polarized single-crystal organic light-emitting devices with low turn-on voltage and high brightness
abstract: We develop linearly-polarized single-crystal OLEDs, which exhibit a degree of polarization of 0.95 for electroluminescence, a turn-on voltage of 2.5 V, an exceptionally high brightness of 200 000 cd m −2 , and a current density surpassing 300 A cm −2 .

title: Water-induced phase separation enables underwater adhesion far exceeding dry adhesion
abstract: Underwater adhesion strength dramatically surpasses that of dry adhesion, which attributes to the surface anchoring effect of nanoparticles formed in situ from the local precipitation of hydrophobic elastomers in the glue induced by water.

title: A novel thickness-gradient electrospun nanomesh for interface-free e-skin applications
abstract: A seamless thickness-gradient nanomesh eliminates abrupt interfaces between soft e-skin and rigid interconnectors. This continuous concept enhances mechanical and electrical durability, and integration with textiles for next-generation wearables.

title: Electro-switchable cellulose nanocrystal films with chiroptical properties
abstract: Electro-switchable chiral nematic films are developed by functionalizing CNCs with viologen derivatives. The films show electrochromic and chiroptical responses, and multi-responsiveness to heat, light, and base for smart optical applications.

title: Highly adaptable oxidative upcycling of polyolefins to multifunctional chemicals containing oxygen and nitrogen
abstract: Highly adaptable upcycling of waste polyolefins was demonstrated to obtain high-value nitro-containing polycarboxylic acids in high carbon yields.

title: Development of novel multi-responsive 4D printed smart nanocomposites with polypyrrole coated iron oxides for remote and adaptive transformation
abstract: A novel multi-responsive nanocomposite integrates polypyrrole-coated magnetic nanoparticles into a thermo-responsive shape memory polymer, enabling precise, remotely dynamic control for 4D-printed biorobotic applications.

title: Mitigating the P2–O2 phase transition-induced structural instability in P2-Na x [Ni 1/3 Mn 2/3 ]O 2 Na-ion battery cathodes: to dope or not to dope, that is the question
abstract: Na 2/3 [Ni 1/3 Mn 2/3 ]O 2 (NNMO) Na-ion battery cathodes cycle well between 2 and 4 V (a) but suffer from severe decay when cycled above 4 V (b) due to P2–O2 transition. Doping strategies used to overcome this issue are discussed in this article.

title: Graphene-based electrochemical sensors for antibiotics: sensing theories, synthetic methods, and on-site monitoring applications
abstract: The successful construction of objective sensors needs to focus on the theoretical elaboration of sensing mechanisms and practical applications for on-site monitoring antibiotics.

title: Synergistic dual-layer passivation boosts efficiency and stability in perovskite solar cells using naphthol sulfonate
abstract: Synergistic dual-layer passivation enhances the quality of film formation, improves interlayer contact and optimizes energy level arrangement.

title: Atomically dispersed Ru in ZIF-67 as a high-performance HER catalyst: in situ structural evolution and deactivation mechanism elucidation
abstract: We implant atomically dispersed noble metals (Ru, Rh, and Pd) into the intact ZIF-67 framework and investigate their HER catalytic performance.

title: Natural wood with optimal capillary water content and evaporation enthalpy for efficient interfacial solar steam generation
abstract: A wood-based evaporator with an efficient evaporation rate of 3.4 kg m −2 h −1 was fabricated by increasing the capillary water content and reducing the evaporation enthalpy of natural wood.

title: MXene-based electrocatalysts for CO 2 reduction: advances, challenges, and perspectives
abstract: The electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide (CO 2 ) is a crucial step toward a sustainable carbon economy, enabling the conversion of greenhouse gases into valuable fuels and chemicals.

title: Advancing endosomal escape of polymeric nanoparticles: towards improved intracellular delivery
abstract: This review explores mechanisms of endosomal escape in polymeric nanoparticles and highlights emerging quantification methods. A critical discussion on current challenges and future strategies for optimizing intracellular delivery is provided.

title: High thermoelectric power factor in Ni–Fe alloy for active cooling applications
abstract: The Seebeck coefficient of Ni–Fe, the metallic alloy proposed for active cooling applications, shows a higher Seebeck coefficient compared to its constituent elements and demonstrates agreement between ML predictions and experimental results.

title: Synthesis of single-atom catalysts with reactive oxygen species-scavenging activity via the dynamic crystal structure transition of coordination polymers
abstract: Single-atom catalysts (SACs) have emerged as a research hotspot in catalysis due to their near-100% atomic utilization efficiency and unique electronic structures.

title: Recycling and regeneration of failed layered oxide cathode materials for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: With broad usage of lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) in electronic devices and electric vehicles (EVs), a large number of decommissioned LIBs will be generated, which cause serious environmental pollution and waste of resources.

title: A chessboard-like photothermoelectric cement cell: a new design for scalable and high efficiency solar energy conversion
abstract: A scalable, low-cost photothermoelectric (PTE) cell using cementitious blocks in a chessboard-like design with varying solar absorbance achieves up to 1.81 × 10 6 μJ m −2 and ∼10 mV K −1 Seebeck coefficient—among the highest for PTE materials.

title: Complex phase transition and asymmetric ferroelectricity of homochiral secondary ammonium salts
abstract: Asymmetric ferroelectricity arising from asymmetric assembly has been observed in a series of homochiral secondary ammonium salts, expanding the understanding of ferroelectricity and inspiring new applications of ferroelectric materials.

title: Reconfigurable bipolar transistors enabled by breathing-Kagome Nb 3 Cl 8
abstract: Novel dual-gate Nb 3 Cl 8 FETs enable programmable bipolar conduction for neuromorphic computing.

title: Rational design of uniform SiO 2 -based afterglow microparticles for photonic crystals
abstract: This article highlights recent advances in the rational design of uniform afterglow SiO 2 microparticles, facilitated by pseudomorphic transformation-assisted doping, identifies key challenges, and explores opportunities in this field.

title: Synergistic enhancement of luminescence and ferroelectricity driven by ( Z )-clipping of a tetraphenylethene
abstract: Synergistic enhancement of luminescence and ferroelectricity (SELF) was explored in a ( Z )-isomer of tetraphenylethene derivatives containing two clipping units in ( Z )-configuration (TPC2-(Z)).

title: Spider-silk inspired ultrafast alkali-induced molecular aggregation for 3D printing arbitrary tubular hydrogels
abstract: Fabricating tubular hydrogel models with arbitrary structural complexity and controllable diameters using an ultrafast, facile yet universal method is desirable for vascular prototypes yet still a great challenge.

title: Significantly enhanced energy harvesting performance in lead-free piezoceramics via a synergistic design strategy
abstract: A synergistic design strategy is proposed to improve the output performance of piezoelectric energy harvester, and the harvester developed by this strategy is successfully applied to train stability monitoring and micro equipment power supply.

title: Synergistic effects of carbon dots and heterojunctions to enable Li–Fe–F all-solid-state ceramic batteries with high cathode loading and cumulative capacity
abstract: A carbon dot-decorated ZnOHF composite interlayer is designed to enable garnet symmetric cells with ultra-long cycling and small overpotential. A Li–Fe–F all-solid-state ceramic battery is achieved with high cathode loading and cumulative capacity.

title: Enhanced stimuli-responsive phase change gels through pickling-enabled ion permeation
abstract: A bioinspired ion-permeation strategy for hydrated salt phase change gels enhances mechanical strength, thermal energy storage, and supercooling stability, with promising applications in deicing and thermal management.

title: Crystalline porous membrane devices: emerging architectures for carbon-neutral technologies
abstract: In this review, we present a comprehensive analysis of crystalline porous materials (MOFs, COFs, and HOFs) as membrane devices for carbon-neutral technologies.

title: Dirac-cone induced metallic conductivity in Cu 3 (HHTP) 2 : high-quality MOF thin films fabricated via ML-driven robotic synthesis
abstract: Machine learning-guided robotic synthesis enables fabrication of high-quality Cu 3 (HHTP) 2 SURMOFs exhibiting intrinsic metallic conductivity—marked by increasing conductivity upon cooling—paving the way for investigating quantum phenomena in 2D MOFs.

title: Sprayed hyaluronic acid based multidrug composite hydrogel for postoperative colorectal cancer ultra-efficient long-lasting multi-stage immuno-chemo synergistic therapy
abstract: We propose a strategy of photothermal-induced ultra-efficient and long-lasting, multi-stage immuno-chemo synergistic therapy for postoperative colorectal cancer using ICP@PDA NPs@composite hydrogels, ensuring a 100% survival rate without recurrence.

title: A dual-mode self-cleaning film for durable passive radiative cooling and solar heating
abstract: A dual-mode thermal management film can switch between heating and radiative cooling modes as required. Its superhydrophobicity on both sides provides excellent anti-fouling ability, adapting to diverse outdoor environments.

title: From 3D to 4D printing of lignin towards green materials and sustainable manufacturing
abstract: This review summarized lignin/lignocellulose materials for various 3D printing techniques and their related applications, where lignin is used in an isolated state, as part of lignocellulose biomass or chemically modified to better bind with a matrix.

title: Self-damping photonic crystals with differentiated reversible crosslinking domains for biomimetic delayed visual perception of underwater impact stress
abstract: Inspired by the octopus’ sucker, we proposed self-damping photonic crystals with differentiated reversible crosslinking domains, which can delayed-release entropic elasticity in water and visually perceive stress field evolution via structural color.

title: Multifunctional miRNA delivery systems provide synergistic neuroprotection against cerebral ischemia reperfusion injury
abstract: A combination of ceria nanozymes and one of metal organic frameworks (MOFs), ZIF-90 nanoparticles delivers miR-210 inhibitors to address challenges of miRNA degradation and effective delivery in therapy of cerebral ischemia-reperfusion (I/R) injury.

title: Machine learning in biosignal analysis from wearable devices
abstract: This review outlines key machine learning algorithms for biosignal analysis and offers guidance on model selection. Applications in neural, cardiovascular, and biochemical signal monitoring for health tracking and disease prediction are discussed.

title: Polyelectrolyte complex-based materials for separations: progress, challenges and opportunities
abstract: Polyelectrolyte complex (PEC) based materials could provide a sustainable alternative to conventional materials, especially for separation applications.

title: Reversal of chirality in solutions and aggregates of chiral tetrachlorinated diperylene diimides towards efficient circularly polarized light detection
abstract: Chiral induction in helicene-based material 4CldiPDI enables highly sensitive circularly polarized light detection in organic phototransistors.

title: Recent progress in perovskite photodetectors: from carrier dynamics to device structures and applications
abstract: Types and applications of perovskite photodetectors.

title: Stabilizing low symmetry-based functions of materials at room temperature through isosymmetric electronic bistability
abstract: We report on an unusual freezing of molecular positions upon heating. Such ascending symmetry-breaking phenomena pave the way to high-temperature applications of devices based on ferroelectric, ferroelastic or ferromagnetic functions of materials.

title: A reflection on ‘Controlled synthesis of conjugated random copolymers in a droplet-based microreactor’
abstract: Heeney et al. reflect on one of their first Materials Horizons papers ( Mater. Horiz. , 2014, https://doi.org/10.1039/C3MH00066D) published in the journal and discuss how their work may have influenced the research field.

title: Solar photons beyond the band gap wavelengths: their effect on solution-processed solar cells
abstract: A full-spectrum optical, thermal, and electrical study (∼300–2500 nm) of high-efficiency organic and perovskite single junction and tandem solar cells, reveals how parasitic absorption and conversion losses impact cells’ temperature and performance.

title: Construction of an Fe–Ni energy bridge for NIR-II luminescence enhancement and anti-thermal quenching via microwave-induced defect engineering
abstract: The demonstration of Cr 3+ -free NIR-II sensitization via Fe 3+ –Ni 2+ energy transfer and microwave defect engineering for improving phosphor thermal stability.

title: Hot carrier diffusion-assisted ideal carrier multiplication in monolayer MoSe 2
abstract: The ideal carrier multiplication efficiency (100%) was observed in monolayer MoSe 2 , achieving the theoretical quantum limit. This breakthrough, driven by ballistic hot-carrier transport, paves the way for next-generation optoelectronics.

title: Correction: Hydrophobic eutectogels with heterostructure for wearable sensing and undersea alarms
abstract: Correction for ‘Hydrophobic eutectogels with heterostructure for wearable sensing and undersea alarms’ by Wenna Wu et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d5mh01437a.

title: A high recognition accuracy tactile sensor based on boron nitride nanosheets/epoxy composites for material identification
abstract: Boron nitride nanosheets/waterborne epoxy composites were used as friction layers for TENG devices to identify materials with strong electrophilicity, achieving a static identification accuracy of 100% for 8 types of negative materials.

title: Engineering of robust conjugated polymer-based aerogels via surface-initiated polycondensation towards sunlight-driven seawater desalination and uranium extraction
abstract: This work shows a strategy for the preparation of uniform and high stability sp 2 C-CPP-based aerogels to simultaneously enhance their photothermal and photocatalytic performance.

title: Green circular design for unmodified lignin: solvent-free valorization into high-performance covalent adaptable networks
abstract: A solvent- and catalyst-free solubilisation-cross-linking strategy converts unmodified lignin to high bio-based, high-performance CANs with ∼63 MPa tensile strength and excellent recyclability.

title: One-component anti-aging agents
abstract: With broader UV absorption and higher antioxidative capacity than the commercial UV-shielding agent UV326 and antioxidant AO1010, one-component anti-aging agents have the potential to improve polymer photostability and food preservation packaging.

title: Engineering biomimetic bacteria membrane-coated nanoparticles: an emerging anti-infection platform
abstract: Bacterial infections threaten global health. This work highlights bacterial membrane-coated nanoparticles (BMCNs) as safe, multifunctional nanoplatforms for efficient drug delivery, antibacterial applications, and tissue regeneration.

title: Ideal energy-absorbing metamaterials based on self-locking bistable structures
abstract: We propose a novel type of energy-absorbing metamaterial with both a rectangular force–displacement curve for efficient energy absorption and steady-state transition capability for locking.

title: Transforming surgical planning and procedures through the synergistic use of additive manufacturing, advanced materials and artificial intelligence: challenges and opportunities
abstract: Additive manufacturing enables advanced, patient-specific medical products and surgical tools for improved healthcare outcomes.

title: Enzyme-induced liquid-to-solid phase transition of a mitochondria-targeted AIEgen in cancer theranostics
abstract: This cancer theranostics study presents the liquid-to-solid phase transition (LST) of an AIEgen, triggered by enzyme-instructed self-assembly (EISA) to generate red-emissive nanoaggregates that enable visualization and induce apoptosis in cancer cells and spheroids.

title: Multifunctional acoustic and mechanical metamaterials prepared from continuous CFRP composites
abstract: The imperative advance towards achieving “carbon neutrality” necessitates the development of porous structures possessing dual acoustic and mechanical properties in order to mitigate energy consumption.

title: A reflection on ‘Side-chain fullerene polyesters: a new class of high refractive index polymers’
abstract: Tu et al. reflect on one of their first Materials Horizons papers ( Mater. Horiz. , 2014, https://doi.org/10.1039/C3MH00105A) published in the journal and discuss how their work may have influenced the research field.

title: Bioinspired wood-based wedge-shaped surface with gradient wettability for enhanced directional liquid transport and fog harvesting
abstract: Inspired by cactus and beetle, a wedge-shaped wood surface with gradient wettability enabled gravity-defying liquid transport and efficient water harvesting, advancing sustainable energy and liquid management applications.

title: Simultaneous shifts in pressure and electric field boost the caloric response in ferroelectrics
abstract: The unprecedented simultaneous application of pressure and electric field offers novel caloric opportunities in the wide family of ferroelectrics: reduction of required fields, performance improvement and tuning of operating temperature.

title: Precise weight tuning in quantum dot-based resistive-switching memory for neuromorphic systems
abstract: QD-based RRAM is proposed for neuromorphic systems. A HfO 2 layer enhances the resistive switching and UV light reduces the switching voltage. MLC with 4 bits achieved can improve ANN performance.

title: Recent advances in smart responsive hydrogel microspheres for tissue regeneration: preparation, characteristics and applications
abstract: The tissue engineering strategy represents a balanced and dynamic approach that utilizes various tissue cells, bio-scaffolds and bio-agents (such as cells and drugs) to facilitate tissue repair and regeneration.

title: Chiral helix amplification and enhanced bioadhesion of two-component low molecular weight hydrogels regulated by OH to eradicate MRSA biofilms
abstract: With only one hydroxyl group difference, BA and SCU can self-assemble with BBR to form nanofibers with varying helicities. The SCU–BBR hydrogel, having greater helicity, exhibits stronger adhesion and biofilm removal capabilities against MRSA.

title: Grain boundary tuning determines iodide and lithium-ion migration in a solid adiponitrile–LiI molecular crystal electrolyte
abstract: Molecular crystals of adiponitrile (Adpn) and LiI via a simple melting method, which in presence of excess Adpn grain-boundaries exhibit faster Li + migration, higher ionic conductivity and a better voltage stability.

title: Strategic azepine engineering realizes highly efficient and stable blue narrowband light-emitting diodes
abstract: New MR-TADF molecules adopt an azepine strategy and isotope effect via a mild borylation, boosting RISC and enabling efficient, stable OLEDs. A developed TADF assistant dopant further raised the EQE of a blue hyperfluorescence OLED to over 40%.

title: A linearly programmable strategy for polymer elastomer mechanics
abstract: The mechanical properties of polymer elastomer materials, such as strength and ductility, play important roles in a wide range of applications, including the carrying of major equipment and the construction of infrastructure.

title: Unravelling the mechanism of coloration and prolonged discoloration in abnormally thermochromic PDMS nanocomposites
abstract: We report an abnormally thermochromic composite whose discoloration time can extend for 6–8 hours. By revealing its color changing mechanism, we demonstrate that this composite can be used in smart windows and information coding.

title: Multifunctional solar-driven interfacial evaporation system for simultaneous clean water production and high-value-added ion extraction
abstract: This review highlights the potential of solar-driven interfacial evaporation (SIE) technology to simultaneously produce clean water and extract valuable ions from water, addressing both water scarcity and resource recovery challenges.

title: Ethanol-induced ammonium polyphosphate–silver gel paint: breaking the trade-off between conductivity, flame retardancy and adhesion in single-layer functional coatings
abstract: An ethanol-induced ammonium polyphosphate–silver (APP–Ag) gel paint is reported, which possesses excellent conductivity, flame retardancy and adhesion.

title: Puncture-resistant hydrogels with high mechanical performance achieved by the supersaturated salt
abstract: Soaking in a supersaturated salt simultaneously optimizes the mechanical performance and puncture resistance of hydrogels.

title: Control of molecular aggregation structures towards flexible organic photovoltaics
abstract: This review evaluates molecular aggregation structures across scales, elucidates mechanisms for enhancing tensile properties and summarizes methods to regulate structures, which provides critical insights for designing efficient and flexible OPVs.

title: Quantum support vector classifier for phase diagram prediction in quinary systems
abstract: This study represents a novel methodology utilizing a quantum support vector classifier to predict phase diagrams in quinary systems which enhances predictive accuracy beyond classical methods.

title: CO 2 -crosslinked cellulose for radiative-cooling-driven passive thermoelectric devices: one stone, two birds
abstract: CO 2 -crosslinked cellulose (Pulp-CO 2 ) was developed for serving dual purposes as a passive radiative cooling membrane and a thermoelectric ionogel scaffold.

title: Advances and challenges of metal organic frameworks (MOFs) and derivatives in photoelectrocatalytic water splitting
abstract: Photoelectrocatalytic (PEC) hydrogen production technology combines the advantages of photocatalysis and electrocatalysis and utilizes solar energy to drive water splitting, which is a technology for sustainable energy systems.

title: Luminescent 3D chiral hybrid metal-halide perovskites for piezoelectric energy harvesting and ultrasound detection
abstract: A pair of luminescent chiral 3D hybrid metal-halide perovskite piezoelectrics were reported and the polycrystalline film device based on a reported compound exhibits favorable performance in energy harvesting and underwater ultrasound detection.

title: Reduction-immobilizing strategy of polymer-embedded sub-2 nm Cu nanoparticles with uniform size and distribution responsible for robust catalytic reactions
abstract: As a contribution to the field of hybrid materials and catalysis, this work is expected to stimulate further research, offering strategic advantages such as high performance, easy recovery, and reusability.

title: Enhancing stable and high-rate lithium ion storage through multifunctional molecular release in a phosphorus/carbon-bipyridine hybrid anode
abstract: High-content bipyridine sustained release neutralizes hydrofluoric acid and facilitates the desolvation of lithium ions, thereby enhancing the lithium storage performance of phosphorus/carbon hybrid anodes.

title: Heat-resistant bio-based superhydrophobic coating from lycopodium powder skeletons for liquid food residue reduction
abstract: A natural superhydrophobic coating from lycopodium spores and carnauba wax offers simple preparation, superior self-cleaning, heat resistance, and resists viscous liquids (honey, yogurt, etc .), showing potential for food packaging applications.

title: Benign mid-gap halide vacancy states in 2D-bismuth-based halide perovskite microcrystals for enhanced broadband photodetectors
abstract: Iodine vacancies in Cs₃Bi₂Br₃I₅.₂ microcrystals generate benign mid-gap states, extending excitation and enhancing charge transport for efficient broadband photodetection.

title: Unexpected hydrophilicity and bio-selectivity of fluorinated PEEK: enhancing bone regeneration through macrophage and BMSC cooperation
abstract: Implantation is the most common strategy for treating large segment bone defects, while current implants mainly focus on osteogenic activity and early immune responses, ignoring their anti-infective capacity and late-stage immune cooperation.

title: Dynamic bonds transform ion transport mechanisms in polymeric covalent adaptable networks
abstract: Cleavable disulfide bonds enable a switch-gate mechanism, regulating ion transport in a solid polymer electrolyte.

title: Charge carrier transport in two-dimensional benzimidazole-based perovskites
abstract: The charge carrier transport properties of two-dimensional benzimidazolium tin iodide perovskite ((Bn) 2 SnI 4 ) show a hopping mechanism for macroscopic transport and a band mechanism on the nanoscopic scale.

title: Recent advances in garnet-based electrolytes for solid-state lithium metal batteries: interfacial challenges and engineering strategies
abstract: In this review, we systematically analyze the interfacial challenges between garnet-type electrolytes/composite electrolytes and electrodes, mainly focusing on the interfacial failure mechanism of garnet-type based SSLMBs.

title: Hydrophilic sp 2 -carbon conjugated co-porphyrin covalent organic frameworks for efficient electrocatalytic oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: A hydrophilic CoT-sp 2 C-P-COF-COOH catalyst bearing strong electron/proton extractor was developed, which may realize continuous electron extraction and fast proton transport and facilitate the electrocatalytic O 2 -to-H 2 O conversion.

title: High-throughput development of tough metallic glass films
abstract: An experimental framework for high-throughput development of tough metallic glass films as strain-sensing materials for application in the field of soft electronics was proposed.

title: Critical role of pore size on perfluorooctanoic acid adsorption behaviors in carbonaceous sorbents
abstract: This study uses contrast matched small angle neutron scattering and simulations to explore how increased pore size in carbon sorbents influences perfluorooctanoic acid adsorption and aggregation, facilitating semi-cylinder micelle formation.

title: The emergence of metal-free molecular perovskites: challenges and opportunities
abstract: This review discusses the challenges associated with the emerging all-organic perovskite materials while outlining opportunities for their further development and applications in the future.

title: Decoding halide perovskites for neuromorphic and memristive devices
abstract: Bridging biological neural systems and halide perovskite-based neuromorphic hardware for next-generation intelligent applications.

title: Molecular engineering of backbone rotation in an energy-dissipative hydrogel for combining ultra-high stiffness and toughness
abstract: A “Hofmeister effect induced Arrested chain Rotation and energy Dissipation” (HARD) strategy unlocks unprecedented synergy of ultra-high stiffness and toughness in hydrogels.

title: Quantum anomalous Hall effect in a nonmagnetic bismuth monolayer with a high Chern number
abstract: The quantum anomalous Hall effect (QAHE) with a high Chern number hosts multiple dissipationless chiral edge channels, which is of fundamental interest and promising for applications in spintronics.

title: Injectable scaffolds with a hierarchically porous structure and augmented paracrine activity for minimally invasive precision medicine
abstract: An injectable 3D-bioprinted scaffold with two-phase emulsion bioink-engineered MSCs exhibits shape memory properties for in situ injection and the augmented paracrine function of MSCs for innervated and vascularized skin regeneration.

title: High- k organic–inorganic hybrid dielectric material for flexible thin-film transistors and printed logic circuits
abstract: A new photopolymerizable organic–inorganic (O–I) hybrid sol–gel material, AUP@SiO x -184, has been synthesized and utilized as a gate dielectric in flexible organic thin-film transistors (OTFTs).

title: A triradical-containing trinuclear Pd( ii ) complex: spin-polarized electronic transmission, analog resistive switching and neuromorphic advancements
abstract: Unveiling a trinuclear Pd( ii ) 3 complex comprising of three ligand-centered radicals and its applicability as a memristor for low power neuromorphic computation and the artificial neural network (ANN) simulated efficient pattern recognition.

title: Conjugated nanofibrous organic cathodes with high-density carbonyl/imine redox sites for superior NH 4 + /H + co-storage
abstract: Conjugated nanofibrous organic (CFO) cathodes with high-density carbonyl/imine redox sites and ultralow reaction energy barriers achieve superior NH 4 + /H + costorage, including high capacity, high-rate capability, and durable lifespan.

title: Controllable transformation of UCST and LCST behaviors in polyampholyte hydrogels enabled by an association–disassociation theory-based switch mechanism
abstract: “Association–disassociation” theory was proposed to offer a controllable mechanism for switching between UCST and LCST behaviors in polyampholyte hydrogels.

title: Novel scalable synthesis of luminescent and magnetic single crystal garnets
abstract: This letter reports a novel method for synthesizing RE 3 Ga 5 O 12 , RE 3 Al 5 O 12 , and RE 3 Fe 5 O 12 single-crystal garnets, where RE refers to rare earth elements, including Sc and Y.

title: Silver-purine MOFs for high-performance multi-terminal neuromorphic memory
abstract: With porous channels for ion transport, the electronic conductivity of MOFs can be tuned to realize outstanding memory operation and seamlessly integrated into devices emulating complex multi-terminal synaptic responses.

title: Oxidation upcycling of polyethylene into degradable, recyclable and high-performance materials
abstract: Polyethylene waste was upcycled into sustainable materials with enhanced strength, processability, degradability/recyclability, and filler compatibility through oxidation–degradation and subsequent dynamic cross-linking reconstruction.

title: Suppressing thermal degradation of CsPbBr 3 quantum dot/EVA films via APTES-mediated interface engineering: toward efficient and stable luminescence down-shifting for silicon solar cells
abstract: A strategy to enhance the thermal stability of CsPbBr 3 PQD-based LDS films for silicon solar cells by capping PQDs with 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane.

title: Is high specific surface area essential for anode catalyst supports in proton exchange membrane water electrolysis?
abstract: Tuning the support surface area optimizes the electrode structure and mass transport, boosting performance and durability.

title: Electrically conductive nanomaterial-enhanced membranes for wastewater reclamation: mechanisms and performance insights
abstract: This review article discusses membrane technology for wastewater reclamation using electrically conductive nanomaterials (ECNMs) and emphasizes “green synthesis” for nanomaterial manufacturing.

title: A multifunctional cardanol-based room-temperature phosphorescent material with multi-stimulus-responsive shape-memory for anti-counterfeiting and encryption
abstract: A sustainable cardanol-based RTP with multi-responsive shape-memory is achieved by integrating rigidity with dynamic networks. It shows a 12 s afterglow, retains stable RTP in H 2 O, HCl and NaOH, and enables programmable 2D/3D anti-counterfeiting.

title: Research on advanced photoresponsive azobenzene hydrogels with push–pull electronic effects: a breakthrough in photoswitchable adhesive technologies
abstract: This study presents azobenzene-based hydrogels for photo-controlled, reversible adhesion. The ABOMe ionic hydrogel exhibits strong adhesion, self-healing, and underwater capabilities, advancing the field of smart biomedical and wearable adhesives.

title: Knitting-stitching bifacial metafabrics with switchable thermal and moisture transmissibility for multimodal dynamic personal thermoregulation
abstract: A hierarchically structured metafabric featuring asymmetrical spectral emissivity and wettability was developed by integrating the tuck knit and rib knit stitches for efficient personal thermal and moisture management.

title: Non-centrosymmetric structures designed rationally via a “dimensionality addition” strategy toward the promising nonlinear optical family [A–X][In–Se] (A = K/Ba and Rb/Ba; X = Cl and Br)
abstract: Six infrared nonlinear optical materials with promising performances were successfully designed using a “dimensionality addition” strategy.

title: Empowering soft conductive elastomers with self-reinforcement and remarkable resilience via phase-locking ions
abstract: This work reports a self-reinforcing ion-conductive elastomer with an ultimate strength of ∼51.0 MPa and an instant resilient efficiency of ∼92.9%, which can be utilized for grid-free position recognition sensors.

title: How does goldene stack?
abstract: The recent synthesis of goldene, a 2D atomic monolayer of gold, has opened new avenues in exploring novel materials.

title: Geometric control of multi-resonance backbone DABNA for narrowband deep-blue electroluminescence
abstract: A novel multi-resonance emitter, c-DABNA, was developed by planarizing the molecular geometry of the backbone DABNA, demonstrating promising deep blue emission with CIE coordinates of (0.161, 0.022) in toluene and (0.156, 0.033) in devices.

title: Poly(3-hexylthiophene) nanoparticles as visible-light photoinitiators and photosensitizers in 3D printable acrylic hydrogels for photodynamic therapies
abstract: Smart light-responsive 3D printable hydrogels were developed by using poly(3-hexylthiophene) nanoparticles as both visible light photoinitiators and photosensitizers to trigger the overproduction of ROS for photodynamic therapies.

title: Enhanced biochemical sensing using metallic nanoclusters integrated with metal–organic frameworks (NCs@MOFs): a comprehensive review
abstract: Significant advancements have been made in biochemical sensing, particularly in the design, development, and utilization of metallic nanoclusters and metal–organic frameworks as separate functional materials.

title: Structuring 3D-printed polypropylene composites with vertically aligned mesophase pitch-based carbon fibers for enhanced through-plane thermal conductivity and mechanical properties
abstract: Vertically aligned structures in thermally conductive polymer-based composites (TPMCs) present an efficient tool for managing heat dissipation in battery packs and the central processing unit (CPU).

title: Structural relaxation chirality transfer enhanced circularly polarized luminescence in heteronuclear Ce III –Mn II complexes
abstract: A new mechanism of structural relaxation chirality transfer was proposed to address the amplified circularly polarized luminescence in Ce III –Mn II complexes.

title: Scratch-resistant sapphire nanostructures with anti-glare, anti-fogging, and anti-dust properties
abstract: Sapphire nanostructures with anti-glare, anti-fogging, and anti-dust properties can exhibit mechanical modulus and hardness comparable to those of bulk glass and scratch-resistant metals. Micrographs of structures before and after scratch tests.

title: Multi-gradient energy-saving smart windows with thermo-response and multimodal thermal energy storage
abstract: Based on high specific heat absorption rate and phase-transition heat absorption performance, “MGES smart window” successfully realizes the characteristics of multi-stage energy saving and heat storage, passive adaptive adjustment, etc .

title: Thermally tailoring dielectric genes of graphene hybrids for tuning electromagnetic properties
abstract: Thermally tailoring dielectric genes are proposed for tuning electromagnetic properties of graphene hybrids. Dielectric genetic engineering is utilized to design diversified graphene hybrids, which can be tuned in extreme temperature environments.

title: High spin–orbit torque efficiency induced by engineering spin absorption for fully electric-driven magnetization switching
abstract: The spin absorption of the CoGd layer spontaneously formed at the Co/Gd interface enhances spin-dependent transport in Pt/Co/Gd/Ir multilayers.

title: A broadband polarization-sensitive photodetector and an infrared encoder based on high crystallinity 1D Bi 2 (Se,S) 3 ternary nanowires
abstract: The Bi 2 (Se,S) 3 ternary nanowire photodetector shows excellent broadband photoelectric performance, polarization-sensitive detection and imaging ability. Moreover, the device exhibits an NPC phenomenon which is used to simulate the infrared encoder.

title: CNFs/Al 2 O 3 –SiC SBD flexible e-skin to achieve dual function sensing of pressure and temperature
abstract: A sandwich-structured dual-mode electronic skin using electrospinning achieves interference-free pressure–temperature sensing for intelligent interaction and wearable health monitoring.

title: Applications, performance enhancement strategies and prospects of Ni x P y in electrocatalysis
abstract: The applications and performance enhancement strategies of Ni x P y with different crystal phases in the field of electrocatalysis are discussed.

title: Bulk thermally conductive polyethylene as a thermal interface material
abstract: We developed a fabrication method to achieve a record-high thermal conductivity of 13.5 W m −1 K −1 for electrically insulated polyethylene at a coverage area of 2.16 mm 2 .

title: Hypoxia-amplifying polymer nanoprodrugs for sonodynamic chemotherapy for breast cancer and bone metastasis via in situ thrombogenesis
abstract: A hypoxia-amplifying polymer nanoprodrug (DPTT) is reported for breast cancer and bone metastasis therapy via in situ thrombogenesis to improve the chemotherapy effect.

title: Enhanced photocatalytic gold recovery with concurrent near-IR fluorescence turn-on sensing: N,S-doped CDs in functionalized dendritic silica as a dual-mode platform
abstract: This study demonstrates the strategic use of nitrogen and sulfur co-doped carbon dots (N,S-CDs) as both a photocatalyst and a near-infrared (NIR) fluorescent probe for the simultaneous adsorption and detection of gold.

title: Reimagining drug nanocarriers: clinical realities and smarter strategies for targeted drug delivery
abstract: Lost in translation: no targeted nanoparticle has reached the clinic due to poor targeting efficacy, and overcoming this will require rethinking pharmacokinetics, optimizing dosing regimens, and addressing issues like antigen depletion.

title: Layered solid Brønsted acid for dynamic interfacial pH regulation toward durable zinc anodes
abstract: Layered solid Brønsted acid HNbMoO 6 ·H 2 O regulates interfacial pH via OH − adsorption and homogenizes ionic flux, preventing byproduct formation and offering oriented Zn 2+ deposition, thus enabling a durable zinc anode for aqueous zinc-ion batteries.

title: Recent progress of gas sensors based on perovskites
abstract: The review examines the recent progress in gas sensors with different perovskite structures and offers suggestions for future research.

title: Ultra-stretchable, self-recoverable, notch-insensitive, self-healable and adhesive hydrogel enabled by synergetic hydrogen and dipole–dipole crosslinking
abstract: This work presents a hydrogel with a dual crosslinked network of hydrogen bonds and dipole–dipole interactions, achieving 9000% stretchability, self-healing, adhesion and conductivity, making it ideal for wearable sensors and bioelectronics.

title: Autonomous self-healing and superior tough polyurethane elastomers enabled by strong and highly dynamic hard domains
abstract: Hard domains composed of dense hydrogen bonds and π–π interactions endow elastomers with autonomous self-healing and excellent mechanical robustness.

title: A bioactive multifunctional dressing with simultaneous visible monitoring of pH values and H 2 O 2 concentrations for promoting diabetic wound healing
abstract: A pH and H 2 O 2 -monitoring dressing (MoO 3− x –EuBG–SA) composed of sodium alginate, EuBG particles and MoO 3− x nanosheets was successfully prepared for diabetic wound management and accelerating wound healing.

title: Janus metal-enabled tunable Schottky barriers in van der Waals contacts via interfacial polarization modulations
abstract: We propose a novel and universal strategy by utilizing polar vdW Janus metals with broken out-of-plane mirror symmetry to achieve adjustable Schottky barriers in metal–semiconductor contacts.

title: Flexible solid–liquid nanocomposite with high surface resistivity for effective electromagnetic interference shielding and heat dissipation
abstract: This work introduces a sequentially bi-continuous solid–liquid nanocomposite (S-PBLM/MLM) that integrates triple functionalities: electrical insulation, EMI shielding and thermal dissipation.

title: Moisture-responsive ultralow-hysteresis polymer ionogels for adhesion-switchable strain sensing
abstract: Adhesion-switchable ionogels with ultralow hysteresis are developed through moisture-mediated phase separation using opposite-affinity ionic components. These ionogels show great potential in smart adhesives for targeted object capture.

title: Silicon negative electrodes for lithium-ion batteries: challenges, advances, and future prospects
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive analysis of silicon-based negative electrodes for next-generation lithium-ion batteries.

title: Inverse design of isotropic auxetic metamaterials via a data-driven strategy
abstract: This study presents a data-driven strategy for designing isotropic auxetic metamaterials using Kolmogorov–Arnold networks and genetic algorithms. The optimized model achieves 0.05% average MSE, outperforming original and perturbed models.

title: Photonic crystal-based structural color switches
abstract: This review categorizes PC-based structural color switches into four distinct approaches, establishing a mechanistic framework to analyze their stimulus-responsive behaviors and applications in sensors, anticounterfeiting, and display.

title: Integrated thermal management-sensing-actuation functional artificial muscles
abstract: A fluid pump and sensing layer for artificial muscle thermal management and real-time monitoring is introduced to reduce cooling time by 90%, triple power density, enhance actuation frequency, and broaden applications by integrating smart composites.

title: Achieving ultrahigh charge–discharge efficiency and energy storage in high-temperature polar polymeric dielectrics via restrained dipole interactions
abstract: Restraining dipole interactions can hinder the formation of conductive pathways in polar polymers, reducing conduction losses and creating deeper charge traps. This enables superior efficiency, high energy density, and enhanced cycling reliability.

title: Boosting thermal conductivity of boron nitride incorporated polymer composites via hydrogen bonding engineering
abstract: Non-covalent hydrogen bonding dramatically boosts thermal transport in boron-nitride polymer composites, revealing an inverse, quantitative relationship between hydrogen-bond density and interfacial thermal resistance.

title: Layered double hydroxide modified bismuth vanadate as an efficient photoanode for enhancing photoelectrochemical water splitting
abstract: This review highlights the functions of layered double hydroxide-modified bismuth vanadate photoanodes in solar water splitting. Future outlook on the photoanode is also summarized.

title: A multi-responsive 3D deformable soft actuator with tunable structural color enabled by a graphene/cholesteric liquid crystal elastomer composite
abstract: Multi-responsive 3D deformable soft actuators with vivid structural color-changing ability are realized through the RGO deposition-induced Janus structures of CLCEs.

title: The search for superionic solid-state electrolytes using a physics-informed generative model
abstract: SHAFT-Density generates novel superionic solid-state electrolyte candidates by integrating crystallographic symmetry and a density-guided strategy. SHAFT-Density identified promising new Li-based materials and showed potential for material discovery.

title: Single ultrabright fluorescent silica nanoparticles can be used as individual fast real-time nanothermometers
abstract: Ultrabright fluorescent ratiometric all-optic nanothermometers.

title: Extracting disorder parameters from optical spectra of non-fullerene acceptors
abstract: Excitonic disorder in NFA thin films is investigated through temperature-dependent optical spectroscopy, linking molecular design to disorder parameters.

title: Porphyrinic metal–organic frameworks as separation membranes: from synthesis to advanced applications
abstract: TCPP MOFs, characterized by diverse nanostructured morphologies, can be integrated into various membranes, performing advanced separation applications.

title: Semiconductor-mediated radiosensitizers: progress, challenges and perspectives
abstract: The principles, enhancement strategies, progress of different semiconductor types, challenges/perspectives, and guidance for commercial applications are systematic reviewed for the semiconductor radiosensitizers.
