title: Perspective on water electrolysis for ozone production: electrocatalyst design and development
abstract: Other than H 2 and O 2 , water electrolysis can also be used for producing ozone for versatile applications. Here we report the recent advances on the relevant catalysts, categorized by material synthesis and types.

title: Beyond the four core effects: revisiting thermoelectrics with a high-entropy design
abstract: Beyond conventional design: unlocking thermoelectric potential for recuperating low-exergy industrial waste heat through high-entropy strategies and data-driven insights.

title: Research progress on clay-based hybrid proton exchange membranes for fuel cells
abstract: Recent advances in clay-based proton exchange membranes are reviewed, focusing on microstructural design and regulation of proton transport channels.

title: Development of an injectable salicylic acid–choline eutectic hydrogel for enhanced treatment of periodontitis
abstract: A DES-CS/GP hydrogel was developed for periodontitis, showing injectability, antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, good gingival permeability and biocompatibility. It reduced bone loss, bacterial loads, and cytokine expression in rat.

title: Mitigating triplet loss in 2D WSe 2 /non-fullerene heterostructures using halogenated acceptors
abstract: Two-dimensional transition metal dichalcogenides (2D TMDCs) can be combined with organic semiconductors to form hybrid van der Waals heterostructures.

title: Synergistic peptide–organic matrix enhances mineralization of biomimetic scaffolds for bone regeneration
abstract: A peptide–matrix co-regulation strategy enhances in situ mineralization and bone regeneration by increasing mineral content and strength, activating BMP2/Smad signaling, and unveiling a SIM-based mechanism for controlling mineralization.

title: Interfacial chemistry at solid–liquid van der Waals heterojunctions enabling sub-5 nm Ohmic contacts for monolayer semiconductors
abstract: Interfacial chemistry at metal/water/monolayer semiconductor van der Waals heterojunctions is investigated by first principles calculations, revealing dipole-induced Fermi-level pinning and Ohmic contacts with low-resistance tunneling behavior.

title: Hard carbon from a sugar derivative for next-generation sodium-ion batteries
abstract: We report on the synthesis of a hard carbon from hydroxymethylfurfural, a sugar derivative, highlighting its excellent potential as an anode material for next-generation sodium-ion batteries.

title: Symmetry-reduction enhancement of nitrate removal on record-breaking layered yttrium hydroxide adsorbents
abstract: The reduction of crystal symmetry of layered yttrium hydroxides during nitrate adsorption results in superior adsorption capacity and kinetics.

title: Multifunctional polyoxomolybdate ionic liquid coatings for mitigating microbiologically influenced corrosion
abstract: Corrosion of metals and other materials in marine environments poses significant economic, operational, safety, and environmental challenges across the oil and gas industry, the renewable energy sector, and maritime infrastructure.

title: Debonding-on-demand adhesives for recycling and reusing of electronic devices
abstract: Debonding-on-demand adhesives enable the disassembly of multilayer electronic devices under external stimuli, facilitating efficient recycling and reuse of valuable components.

title: Regulating intermolecular interactions for stable multifunctional organic–inorganic metal halide hybrid glasses
abstract: A new concept of modulating intramolecular interactions was proposed to achieve high glass-formation ability and tunable crystallization in OIMH glass.

title: Rational design of redox active metal organic frameworks for mediated electron transfer of enzymes
abstract: The first study to rigorously investigate the design and application of redox active MOFs (raMOFs) in enzyme-based bioelectrochemistry.

title: The van der Waals antiferromagnetic proximity effect at the FePS 3 /Pt uncompensated interface
abstract: The van der Waals antiferromagnetic proximity effect in FePS 3 /Pt heterostructures is experimentally demonstrated, where interfacial Fe vacancies play a key role in inducing localized magnetic moments and enhancing exchange interactions.

title: Conjugated polymer nanoparticles boosting growth and photosynthesis in biohybrid plants
abstract: Engineered nanomaterials integrated into photosynthetic systems could pave the way to new, exciting avenues towards biohybrid systems and renewable energy sources.

title: Polyimide-driven innovations as “inert” components in high-performance lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Polyimide-based “inert” components in LIBs include separators, solid-state electrolytes, protective layers, and binders.

title: Research progress on conductive polymer-based microwave absorption materials: from materials design to functionalities and applications
abstract: Composition, structures, functionalities, and applications of conductive polymer-based microwave absorption materials.

title: Supramolecular ionogels enable highly efficient electrochromism
abstract: Supramolecular ionogels served as gel electrolytes for electrochromic devices using electrochromic polymers or viologen derivatives, exhibiting performance comparable to ionic liquid electrolytes and superior to polymer gelator-based ionogels.

title: Regulating Zn 2+ /H + selectivity through functional group design of separators for long-lifespan aqueous zinc batteries
abstract: This work identifies that polyethersulfone effectively regulates the transfer of Zn 2+ , H + , and H 2 O, thereby concurrently suppressing dendrite growth, hydrogen evolution, and corrosion.

title: Reprocessable supramolecular polymer adhesives for on-demand adhesion in multiple scenarios
abstract: A new SPA based on lipoic acid and hydroxypropyl β-cyclodextrin that has tailored adhesion behavior and unique time-temperature dependent characteristics for on-demand adhesion controlled via a simple cyclic heating–cooling strategy is presented.

title: Highly reliable organic light-emitting diodes with optimized fill factor based on rotational membrane design for stretchable displays
abstract: A novel rotational substrate and reliable encapsulation for SOLEDs enable uniform stress distribution up to 60% strain, along with high luminance and durability.

title: Unearthing the emerging properties at buried oxide heterointerfaces: the γ-Al 2 O 3 /SrTiO 3 heterostructure
abstract: Epitaxially combining oxide layers can create new functionalities surpassing those of separate parent materials. This review highlights the unique structural, electronic, and magnetic properties of γ-Al 2 O 3 /SrTiO 3 , revealing the underlying mechanisms.

title: Correction: An immunological electrospun scaffold for tumor cell killing and healthy tissue regeneration
abstract: Correction for ‘An immunological electrospun scaffold for tumor cell killing and healthy tissue regeneration’ by Xingzhi Liu et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2018, 5 , 1082–1091, https://doi.org/10.1039/C8MH00704G.

title: A combined plastic shaping method for powder metallurgy complex multi-material structures
abstract: A cost-effective solution called combined plastic shaping for medium-to-large-scale production of complex multi-material components with broad material compatibility was developed.

title: Proton-controlled Dzyaloshinskii–Moriya interaction and topological Hall effect in hydrogenated strontium ruthenate
abstract: A practically pure proton doping effect is realized to control the topological Hall effect in strontium ruthenate using the catalysis-assisted hydrogenation method.

title: An atomically precise alloy AgCu cuboid nanocluster with a cubic core: gram scale synthesis, total structure, electronic structure, and catalytic performance
abstract: The study presents the total structure and gram scale synthesis of a 32-atom AgCu alloy nanocluster [Ag 4 Cu 28 H 6 (PET) 16 Cl 8 (PPh 3 ) 8 ][BF 4 ] 2 , which shows excellent catalytic activity in the hydrogenation of hazardous nitroarenes.

title: Synergistic solid–liquid hybrid electrolyte for cycle-stable and high-efficiency Li–CuCl 2 batteries
abstract: This innovative solid–liquid hybrid electrolyte synergistically suppresses cathode dissolution and Cu-ion migration for stable Li–CuCl 2 batteries.

title: High current density heterojunction bipolar transistors with 3D-GaN/2D-WSe 2 as emitter junctions
abstract: With the continuous advancement of electronic technology, there is an increasing demand for high-speed, high-frequency, and high-power devices.

title: Three-dimensional morphology in localized Joule heating-induced structured films for enhanced sensitivity in soft pressure sensors
abstract: Manufacturing well-designed three-dimensional (3D) structures on the surface of a soft elastomer is essential for achieving a highly sensitive soft pressure sensor and for pursuing advanced applications.

title: Atomic hysteretic diffusion enables high-strength TiAl/Ni joints via cluster-plus-glue-atom modeled GCFMs
abstract: The development of high-performance Ni/TiAl composite structures demands innovative solutions to overcome strength–ductility synergy.

title: Multivariate modulation of Zr 6 UiO-66 for enhanced cooperative CO 2 adsorption through defect multi-functionalisation
abstract: Multivariate modulation as a new approach to enhance MOFs’ CO 2 adsorption capacity through defect-multifunctionalisation.

title: Emerging multi-functional delafossite materials: frontier advances and prospective breakthroughs in photoelectronic applications
abstract: ABO 2 -type delafossites, distinguished by their layered crystalline framework, tunable quantum-enabled properties, and inherent sustainability, represent an emerging multifunctional material platform for next-generation photoelectronics.

title: Unveiling selectivity trends for CO 2 reduction reaction over Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene: the key role of less-stable intermediate states and coadsorbates
abstract: Less-stable intermediate states open new reaction channels and must be included in the analysis of free-energy diagrams to correctly capture selectivity trends.

title: Perylene diimide-based photocatalysts: from molecular design to emerging applications
abstract: This review systematically introduces the evolution of PDI-based photocatalytic systems, combined with modern synthesis methods and emphasis on structural/functional modifications to advance semiconductor photocatalysis for solar energy harvesting.

title: High-voltage hydrovoltaic generator based on micro/nano multi-scale superhydrophilic SiO 2 @activated carbon with enhanced capillary infiltration performance
abstract: A hierarchically structured, multi-scale hydrovoltaic composite material system is designed for energy harvesting and environmental monitoring.

title: Carbon-based electrodes for photo-bio-electrocatalytic microbial fuel and electrolysis cells: advances and perspectives
abstract: Synergy of carbon-based photo-bio-electrocatalysts and photocatalysis in fuels of the future, MFCs and MECs, is vital for sustainable living. This merging will revolutionize energy systems, paving the way for cleaner and efficient power generation.

title: Chloride ion-capturing La 0.7 Sr 0.3 BO 3 (B = Fe, Co) perovskite oxides achieving superior electrochemical desalination performance
abstract: Perovskite oxides were selected via machine learning as promising Cl − capturing electrode candidates for capacitive deionization, achieving superior desalination performance.

title: Thermogalvanic hydrogels for low-grade heat harvesting and health monitoring
abstract: With the advances and ongoing research efforts discussed in this review, a continuous breakthrough in high-performance thermogalvanic hydrogels for efficient low-grade heat harvesting and wearable electronics for health monitoring is foreseeable.

title: Cross-dimensional assembly of MXene/SiO 2 /KNF composite aerogels for radar and infrared stealth
abstract: This study designs a multifunctional aerogel with a bionic ”root–soil” structure using 0D SiO 2 , 2D MXene, and 1D Kevlar nanofibers, achieving strong microwave loss and infrared suppression for multispectral stealth applications.

title: Interface regulation toward high-performance narrow-bandgap perovskite photodetectors: mechanisms and challenges
abstract: This review highlights recent advances in interface engineering for narrow-bandgap perovskite photodetectors, offering strategies to enhance response speed, stability, and spectral selectivity for low-cost, high-sensitivity near-infrared detection.

title: Recent advances in flexible and wearable OLEDs for biomedical applications: a review
abstract: This review highlights recent advances in flexible OLED technologies tailored for biomedical applications, with emphasis on their current use in phototherapy and prospects in wearable healthcare.

title: Solid-state self carbo-passivation for refurbishing colloidal dispersity of catalytic silica nanoreactors
abstract: A self-carbo-passivation strategy tackles the agglomeration issue in silica nanostructures by thermally forming carbonaceous skin patches on the nanoparticle surface.

title: Stretch-activated morphing enabled by integrated physical–chemical network engineering
abstract: A dynamic covalent strategy is proposed to create elastic and plastic layers and integrate them through robust interfaces, enabling the facile fabrication of reliable composites exhibiting programmable, heterogeneous mechanical responses.

title: Electron density modulation in monolayer MoS 2 through the phase transition of a relaxor ferroelectric substrate
abstract: We demonstrate reversible modulation of electron density in monolayer MoS 2 by exploiting the low-temperature phase transition of a relaxor ferroelectric substrate, offering a substrate-driven tunable doping and hysteretic behavior in 2D materials.

title: Dual surrogate imprinting: an innovative strategy for the preparation of virus-selective particles
abstract: This work involves the preparation of dual surrogate-imprinted polymers (D-MIPs) for the capture of SARS-CoV-2.

title: Research on light-responsive luminescence properties of carbon dots and their applications
abstract: This review classifies light-responsive carbon dots into photoluminescent and photochromic categories, examines their synthesis strategies and optical mechanisms, and discusses the applications of these materials in various fields.

title: Unveiling the electrochemical nitrogen reduction reaction mechanism in heteroatom-decorated-Mo 2 CS 2 –MXene: the synergistic effect of single-atom Fe and heteroatom
abstract: The synergistic effect of a single atom of Fe and heteroatom in X-doped Mo 2 CS 2 –MXene (X = B, N, O, F, P and Se) in enhancing the electrocatalytic reduction of nitrogen to ammonia.

title: Regulating hydrogel mechanical properties with an electric field
abstract: A semi-interpenetrating polymer network hydrogel is designed for electric field-driven mechanical property change via salt transport, without significant shape deformation.

title: Programmable assemblies of photothermal anisotropic micromotors for multimodal motion
abstract: A highly controllable and programmable self-assembly process has been developed, enabling multimodal motion through anisotropic micromotors fabricated using microfluidic technology.

title: Scab-inspired strong adhesive tapes with self-detachment based on water-mediated destruction of polymer crystals
abstract: Strong adhesive tapes with water-triggered self-detachment are designed based on crystallization and destruction of polymer crystals. The tapes show great potential for use in smart adhesives for human motion detection and painless removal.

title: Nanotherapeutics-mediated tolerogenic induction for enabling adeno-associated virus vector gene therapy re-administration by overcoming anti-drug antibodies
abstract: Utilizing co-assembled nanoparticles composed of lipidated rapamycin and itaconic acid to enable repeatable administration of recombinant adeno-associated virus vectors by eliminating the formation of anti-drug antibodies.

title: In situ study of visible light-activated photocatalytic conversion reaction enhancement of biomass using a magnetic heterostructure
abstract: This study showed that the CdS/Fe 3 O 4 heterostructure exhibited superior visible light photocatalytic activity over CdS, while preserving magnetic properties, leading to a 100% recovery rate.

title: Orthogonal three-dimensional manipulation of a chiro-photonic hybrid-architecture enabling high-order information encryption
abstract: Our work achieves the orthogonal three-dimensional manipulation of a chiro-photonic hybrid-architecture, extending the current technologies in manipulating responsive soft materials.

title: Out-of-plane quadrupolar excitons in Ruddlesden–Popper perovskites: theoretical insights into the effects of organic spacer cations
abstract: This work demonstrates out-of-plane quadrupolar characteristic of excitons in two-dimensional Ruddlesden–Popper perovskites, which is attributed to the hydrogen bonding between organic spacer cations and apical I ions of the inorganic octahedron.

title: Light-induced tunable threshold voltage and synaptic behavior of a solution-processed indium oxide thin film transistor for logic computing and image denoising
abstract: The V TH and photo-synaptic behavior of solution-processed amorphous In 2 O 3 TFT has been regulated precisely by employing a simple and reversible annealing-illumination method, resulting in a high gain inverter and impressive image denoising ability.

title: Models connecting microstructure and charge transport in disordered semiconducting polymers: from theories to digital design
abstract: Theoretical methods connecting chemical composition, structure, electronic properties and charge transport properties are reviewed with focus on the approaches used to connect between models at different resolutions.

title: Leveraging solvent affinity for phase-selective doping to enhance doping efficiency in a DPP-based n-type conjugated polymer
abstract: Solvent affinity (higher solvent affinity A and partial solvent affinity B) driven phase-selective doping.

title: In-plane aligned doping pattern in electrospun PEI/MBene nanocomposites for high-temperature capacitive energy storage
abstract: To achieve superior energy storage performance in dielectric polymer films, it is crucial to balance three key properties: high dielectric constant, high breakdown strength, and low dielectric loss.

title: All-electrical layer-spintronics in altermagnetic bilayers
abstract: Electrical manipulation of spin-polarized current is highly desirable for ultracompact spintronic device technology and can be achieved in an altermagnetic bilayer by combining the concepts of altermagnetism and layertronics .

title: Sulfide electrolytes for all-solid-state sodium batteries: fundamentals and modification strategies
abstract: This review provides a summary of the latest advancements in sulfide solid-state electrolytes for all-solid-state sodium batteries. It emphasizes the various preparation methods, modification strategies, and enhancements in interfacial stability.

title: A shape-reconfigurable electronic composite for stimulus customizable detection via neutral plane shifting
abstract: A shape-reconfigurable electronic composite implements a 3D sensor that can selectively detect external force in particular directions, via neutral plane shifting controlled by the properties of a stiffness-tunable polymer.

title: Photoinduced charge separation at heterojunctions between two-dimensional layered materials and small organic molecules
abstract: p–n heterojunctions between 2D layered materials and organic molecules have been developed for better understanding of photoinduced charge separation mechanism and optoelectronics applications ( e.g. , solar cells).

title: Machine learning-assisted X-ray absorption analysis of bimetallic catalysts
abstract: Bimetallic nanoparticles have attracted increasing scientific and technological interest as modules for creating nanoscale materials with unique magnetic, electronic, and chemical properties.

title: Ductile copper hydride Eliashberg superconductors with T c in the liquid-nitrogen temperature range and band topology at ambient pressure
abstract: Through a series of synthesis simulations, cubic Cu 4 H 3 demonstrates band topology, mechanical ductility, and high- T c superconductivity up to 77 K at ambient pressure.

title: Surfactant-based interface capture towards the development of 2D-printed photonic structures
abstract: This study focuses on fabricating photonic crystals (PCs) by surfactant-based particle capture at the gas–liquid interface of evaporating sessile droplets.

title: Emergent multiferroicity in two-dimensional electron gas of complex oxides for FET-based artificial synaptic junctions
abstract: Multifunctional nanoarchitecture: integrating a 2D electron gas with robust multiferroicity enables high-mobility quantum transport and tunable ferroelectric polarization. Paves the way for advanced spintronics and energy-efficient neuromorphic computing.

title: Recent advances in nanomaterial-based adsorbents for removal of pharmaceutical pollutants from wastewater
abstract: Adsorption is widely recognized as an effective and practical approach for mitigating the environmental hazards associated with pharmaceutical waste, owing to its operational simplicity, economic feasibility, and consistent performance.

title: Thermo-responsive 3D nanostructures for enhanced performance in food-poisoning bacterial analysis
abstract: Thermo-URCHANO, a thermo-responsive urchin-like 3D nanopillar substrate, efficiently captures food-poisoning bacteria and releases them through temperature changes, that enhancing bacterial detection sensitivity.

title: Carbon dots gels: synergistic platforms for advanced visual information storage
abstract: This review systematically presents practical strategies for designing and fabricating carbon dot gels, while providing a comprehensive analysis of their applications in visual information storage.

title: Becoming a foodie in virtual environments: simulating and enhancing the eating experience with wearable electronics for the next-generation VR/AR
abstract: Human–machine interfaces have received significant attention for their potential in VR/AR. This review summarizes recent progress in simulating physical and chemical sensations for enhancing eating experiences by utilizing wearable electroncis.

title: Toughening hydrogels through a multiscale hydrogen bonding network enabled by saccharides for a bio-machine interface
abstract: A saccharide-enabled multiscale hydrogen bonding network significantly enhances the overall performance of hydrogel materials. This versatile approach suits diverse material systems and enables stable bioelectronic interfaces.

title: Electric field-dependent thermal conductivity of relaxor ferroelectric PMN-33PT through changes in the phonon spectrum
abstract: Solid-state thermal switches are vital for thermal power generation and cooling, leveraging strain-coupled ferrons in ferroelectrics. In relaxor ferroelectric PMN–PT, we demonstrate up to 10% modulation of thermal conductivity using an E-field.

title: A reflection on ‘Flexibility versus rigidity: what determines the stability of zeolite frameworks? A case study’
abstract: Breynaert, Kirschhock et al. reflect on one of their first Materials Horizons papers ( Mater. Horiz. , 2014, https://doi.org/10.1039/C4MH00127C) published in the journal and discuss how their work may have influenced the research field.

title: Bridging sensing and action: autonomous object sorting by reprogrammable liquid crystal elastomers
abstract: We present a reprogrammable liquid crystal elastomer actuator that integrates sensing, planning, and actuation to autonomously sort objects by green-light transmission. Pattern-encoded responses enable material-based decision-making.

title: Side-chain engineering of conjugated molecules for n-type organic thermoelectrics
abstract: The performance of n-type thermoelectrics has increased significantly over the years, with side chain engineering playing a pivotal role in advancing this field.

title: Boosting the energy storage performance of BCZT-based capacitors by constructing a Schottky contact
abstract: Oxygen vacancies were introduced to form a Schottky contact, redistributing the electric field in the BCZT-based multilayers. This improved the voltage endurance of BCZT/BCZT-OD//(1P), leading to an enhanced W rec .

title: Reactive oxygen species-responsive prodrug nanomicelle-functionalized Lactobacillus rhamnosus probiotics for amplified therapy of ulcerative colitis
abstract: Natural licorice root-derived 18β-glycyrrhetinic acid prodrug-immobilized Lactobacillus rhamnosus is proposed for treating ulcerative colitis and its associated depression-like behaviors.

title: Spontaneous helix formation in a polar smectic phase
abstract: A new type of smectic phase, SmCHP, is presented, with a proper ferroelectric nature and a spontaneously formed heliconical structure. Under the application of an electric field, the helix axis can be reoriented, without disturbing the helical pitch.

title: Bioelectronic building blocks: low-voltage integrable organic thin-film transistors with a tri-layer gate dielectric design
abstract: This study presents a novel tri-layer gate dielectric design for organic thin-film transistors, tailored for bioelectronic applications, with improved yield, uniformity, and integrability for systems.

title: Understanding mechanical failure behaviours and protocol optimization for fast charging applications in Co-free Ni-based cathodes for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Suppressing failure modes and optimizing protocols enable enhanced cyclability and fast chargeability in Co-free, high-Ni layered oxide cathodes.

title: Direct growth of ferroelectric orthorhombic ZrO 2 on Ru by atomic layer deposition at 300 °C
abstract: A CMOS-compatible low temperature ferroelectric orthorhombic ZrO 2 on Ru by atomic layer deposition is developed.

title: Light-to-matter chirality transfer in plasmonics
abstract: Introduction and review of work developing chirality in achiral plasmonic structures using only circularly polarized light to induce asymmetry, with a detailed introduction to chirality and light–matter interaction in molecules and nanostructures.

title: Uncovering internal water-flux and surface-potential dominance in hydrogel-based moisture-enabled power generation: mechanistic insights and performance enhancement
abstract: Principle-oriented investigations show hydrogel-based moisture-enabled generators generate ≈9.3 μA h per g of transported H 2 O, and co-optimizing water migration and internal electric fields boosts output from 0.1 V to 0.93 V.

title: Tailoring a multifunctional polyglutamic acid–tragacanth gum binder for enhancing the lithium storage performance of red phosphorus anodes
abstract: A multifunctional binder for red phosphorus anodes was designed.

title: Polyimide nanocomposites for next generation spacesuits
abstract: Exploring the potential of polyimide–carbon nanocomposites with enhanced mechanical strength, self-healing ability, dust repellence, fire resistance and protection against atomic oxygen for next-generation spacesuits.

title: Ultra-high thermally conductive graphite microplatelet/aramid nanofiber composites with reduced interfacial thermal resistances by engineered interface π–π interactions
abstract: Interfacial π–π interactions help to prepare large-size graphite microplatelets and the corresponding ultra-high thermally conductive composites with significantly reduced filler–filler and filler–matrix interfacial thermal resistances.

title: Mechanical and thermal responsive chiral photonic cellulose hydrogels for dynamic anti-counterfeiting and optical skin
abstract: Using a photo-crosslinking strategy, chiral nematic hydroxypropyl cellulose (HPC) hydrogels with accurately controlled and multi-mode (mechanical and thermal) responsive structural colors were fabricated.

title: In situ synchrotron X-ray diffraction for actuation in sodium bismuth titanate relaxor ferroelectrics
abstract: This work reveals that lattice stretching contributes the largest portion, while direct volume changes play a minor role in the electrostrain by in situ synchrotron X-ray diffraction, offering theoretical insights for optimizing actuator performance.

title: Cu-incorporated NH 2 -MIL-125(Ti): a versatile visible-light-driven platform for enhanced photocatalytic H 2 generation and CO 2 photoconversion
abstract: Here, we present for the first time an efficient platform for simultaneous H 2 generation and CO 2 conversion into HCOOH, utilizing a Cu-incorporated NH 2 -MIL-125(Ti) material with triethanolamine as the sacrificial agent.

title: Heat transport properties of PbTe 1− x Se x alloys using equivariant graph neural network interatomic potential
abstract: The suppression of heat transport in disordered crystals arises from a competition between mass fluctuations and bond disorder, but their relative contributions remain difficult to disentangle.

title: Improving electron injection of organic light-emitting transistors via interface layer design
abstract: The interface layer with interfacial dipole effects is utilized in OLET devices, resulting in enhanced ambipolar properties and excellent air stability.

title: Highly elastic and breathable PTFE/hydrogel hierarchically compounded dressings for multi-functional protection and rapid wound healing
abstract: Accelerating wound healing while reducing patient discomfort remains a major challenge in biomedical engineering.

title: Dual-functional surface coatings integrating antimicrobial and antibiofouling mechanisms: from material design to application landscapes
abstract: Surface-mediated pathogen transmission remains a critical vector for infectious diseases, especially amidst biofilm-associated infections and rising antimicrobial resistance.

title: Multifunctional applications enabled by tunable multi-emission and ultra-broadband VIS-NIR luminescence via energy transfer in Sn 2+ /Mn 2+ -doped lead-free Zn-based metal halides
abstract: Tunable multi-emission and ultra-broadband visible to near-infrared luminescence in Sn 2+ /Mn 2+ -doped lead-free Zn-based metal halides have been achieved by adjusting the doping ratios and excitation wavelengths.

title: Programing optical properties of single-walled carbon nanotubes with benzoyl peroxide derivatives of tailored chemical characteristics
abstract: We demonstrate a highly tunable platform for modification of the optical properties of single-walled carbon nanotubes, strongly enhancing radiative recombination of excitons.

title: Rare earth valve manipulates dual regulation of electronic states and adsorption geometry in the selective hydrogenation of ethynylbenzene
abstract: A PtCoCe alloy enhances ethynylbenzene hydrogenation via d–f orbital coupling and steric effects. Electron redistribution optimizes d-band center, while the Ce with large radius weakens styrene adsorption, thus boosting the activity and selectivity.

title: Organic full-color narrowband afterglow
abstract: Developing a facile and universally applicable strategy to achieve organic full-color narrowband afterglow (OFNA) is highly desirable, as it meets the strategic demands for low-cost and high-color-purity displays in flexible electronics.

title: Retraction: Unity photoluminescence quantum yield and superior stability achieved in lanthanide-doped lead-free Cs 2 NaInCl 6 double perovskites for highly efficient light emitting diodes
abstract: Retraction of ‘Unity photoluminescence quantum yield and superior stability achieved in lanthanide-doped lead-free Cs 2 NaInCl 6 double perovskites for highly efficient light emitting diodes’ by Hamza Shaili et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d5mh00401b.

title: Macroscopic assembly behavior of silk/lignin-based adhesive hydrogels: elementary macroscopic assembly
abstract: Rationally designed hydrogels with moderate adhesiveness for studying macroscopic assembly kinetics illustrate elementary macroscopic assemblies in analogue to elementary reactions.

title: Exploring the wonders of polysaccharides in porous materials
abstract: Porous materials are a class of materials with abundant micro-nano pore structures, low density, light weight, and large specific surface area, and they have been widely used in various fields.

title: The smart valve for micro flow-velocity regulation based on the “Interfacial Barrier” effect of wettability-patterned surfaces
abstract: “Interfacial Barrier” effect on wettability-patterned surfaces and “Smart” valve design. Mechanism of “Interfacial Barrier” via force and drop shape analysis.

title: Hollow carbon nanoreactors integrating NiFe-LDH nanodots with adjacent La single atoms for efficient oxygen electrocatalytic reactions
abstract: Hierarchically porous hollow carbon nanocages confining NiFe-LDH nanodots and La single atoms together constitute an efficient oxygen evolution reaction nanoreactor catalyst with advantages in mass transfer and intrinsic activity.

title: Magnetically responsive intelligent fibers: nano-engineered materials and multifunctional integration for advanced applications
abstract: Magnetically responsive intelligent fibers integrate magnetic materials into flexible fiber architectures, enabling remote actuation, multimodal sensing, and self-powered operation for wearable electronics, robotics, biomedicine, and energy systems.

title: Enhancing oxygen evolution reaction performance via Zn/Fe co-doping in a Co 3 O 4 nanostructure: mechanistic insights and surface reconstruction dynamics
abstract: A Zn/Fe co-doped Co 3 O 4 was constructed by combining an etching treatment with a heteroatom doping process using ZIFs as precursor, which is found to generate highly active FeOOH/CoOOH species on its surface during OER and follow the LOM pathway.

title: From solid to liquid piezoelectric materials
abstract: The piezoelectric phenomena observed in a wide range of materials from crystalline solids to 3D liquids are reviewed. Special emphasis is given to recent measurements of the piezoelectric responses of fluid ferroelectric nematic liquid crystals.

title: Outstanding high-temperature capacitive performance in all-organic dielectrics enabled by synergistic optimization of molecular traps and aggregation structures
abstract: A strategy of molecular trap and aggregation structure optimization is developed to improve the capacitive performance of dielectrics. An all-organic composite achieves a U e of 3.8 J cm −3 with η > 90% and cyclability over 100 000 cycles at 200 °C.

title: Modeling and design of 3D printed hyperelastic lattice metamaterials with bionic S-shaped stress–strain behaviors
abstract: We develop a design framework for hyperelastic lattice metamaterials to achieve programmable S-shaped stress–strain curves, enabling bionic mechanical behaviors and highly stretchable lattice sensors.

title: Efficient direction-independent 3D spiral fog collector
abstract: A novel 3D fog collector combines a spiral structure with superhydrophilic triangular protrusions and a flow-direction-independent wettability gradient for enhanced efficiency.

title: Organic–inorganic hybridisation strategy for synthesizing durable colored superamphiphobic coatings
abstract: Inspired by Maya blue, color superoleophobic coatings were fabricated using silica, metakaolin and azo dyes, exhibiting excellent stability, wettability and color fastness.

title: Cerium-alloyed dendrite-inhibited highly stable anodes for all-solid-state lithium batteries
abstract: The Ce element was introduced into Li–In to form Li–In–Ce alloys, which can clearly suppress the growth of Li–In dendrites according to the in situ observation test, leading to the good cycling stability of the all-solid-state lithium batteries.

title: Dynamically crosslinked polyethylene-like materials with reversible self-reporting properties
abstract: A polyethylene-like thermoset material with reversible self-reporting and self-healing properties was developed. Rhodamine is used as both cross-linker and mechanochromic probe, enabling damage detection and thermal-triggered repair.

title: Synergistic effects in ambipolar blends of mixed ionic–electronic conductors
abstract: A polymer–fullerene blend OMIEC system shows enhanced capacitance, mobility, and response time due to synergistic ionic and electronic transport, driven by microstructural tuning.

title: Multi-scale structural engineering enables thermochromic organic–inorganic hydrogels for robust smart windows
abstract: Thermochromic organic–inorganic hydrogels for robust smart windows.

title: Preserved elastic modulus and enhanced adhesion in long-term and thermally stable glycol gels via affine shrinkage upon water evaporation
abstract: Glycol-based polymer gels have emerged as stable alternatives to water-based hydrogels, offering intrinsic advantages owing to the ultra-low volatility and high thermal stability of glycol solvents.

title: Designing pores to suppress crack generation at the interface between serpentine interconnects and elastomers for highly durable stretchable electronics
abstract: Geometrically engineered pores in polymer-encapsulated serpentine interconnects suppressed interfacial cracking, enabling the use of stretchable devices with enhanced mechanical durability and stable electrical performance.

title: Bio-based derived carbon materials for permittivity metamaterials: dual efficacy of electromagnetic wave protection and Joule heating
abstract: CW@PBD composites with trace PBD loading achieve negative permittivity, enabling superior EMI shielding (39.0 dB), microwave absorption (RL min = −49.2 dB), and Joule heating (255.68 °C at 3.0 V).

title: Hydrogen sulfide-generating semiconducting polymer nanoparticles for amplified radiodynamic–ferroptosis therapy of orthotopic glioblastoma
abstract: A hydrogen sulfide (H 2 S)-generating semiconducting polymer nanoparticle (PFeD@Ang) is developed for amplified radiodynamic–ferroptosis therapy of orthotopic glioblastoma via a combinational action.

title: Chemically configurable analogue memristors, via the chemiresistive response of oxidized MXene
abstract: Chemically modulable chemirestive response of oxidized MXene.

title: Room-temperature synthesis of triple-cation green perovskite quantum dots for optoelectronic applications
abstract: This article provides insight into the synthesis of efficient triple cation perovskite quantum dots for optoelectronics applications.

title: Redox tunable conjugated radicals enable low threshold voltage memristors for artificial synapses
abstract: A series of redox tunable conjugated radicals were developed. The threshold voltages of radical based memristors decrease as positive redox potentials drop and can be as low as 0.48 V, and synaptic plasticity and image recognition are demonstrated.

title: High-performance yet sustainable epoxy composites: from Diels–Alder chemistry to hydrazinolytic degradation
abstract: This study pioneers a one-pot synthesis merging the Diels–Alder reaction with amine-epoxy curing to create sustainable, high-performance composites. Hydrazinolysis enables carbon fiber recovery and monomer recycling, promoting composite circularity.

title: Adaptive ferroelectric memristors with high-throughput BaTiO 3 thin films for neuromorphic computing
abstract: High-performance ferroelectric tunnel junction (FTJ) synapse devices with adaptive conductance compensation from ferroelectric diode (FD) and resistor ( R ) components have been demonstrated on a high-throughput thickness-gradient BaTiO 3 thin film.

title: Humidity-driven modulation of ferroelectricity in hafnia–zirconia membranes
abstract: Intrinsic ferroelectricity in freestanding hafnia membranes can be controlled by its electrochemical state, demonstrating that low-humidity conditions significantly improve ferroelectric properties without requiring a distinct wake-up process.

title: Enhanced crystallization and dielectric properties of atomic layer deposited SrTiO 3 thin films on Ru electrode by inserting GeO 2 interfacial layer
abstract: Densely-deposited STO thin films on GeO 2 /Ru crystallized at the lower temperature, yielding defect-free layer with superior properties for DRAM capacitor applications.

title: Multi-functional smart bulk hydrogel panels with strong near-infrared shielding and active local control
abstract: Smart hydrogel panel with strong near-infrared shielding and active local control.

title: Fast and stable NH 4 + storage in multielectron H-bonding-acceptor organic molecules for aqueous zinc batteries
abstract: Ultrastable lock-and-key H-bonding networks between sextuple-active 2,7-dinitrophenanthraquinone (DNPQ) and tetrahedral NH 4 + charge carriers are designed, endowing the Zn-organic battery with superior rate capability and cyclic life.

title: Fluorobenzylation promotes immune-activating peptides to turn cold tumors into hot tumors
abstract: Fluorobenzylation enhances the immunogenicity and tumor penetration of immune-activating peptides, enabling nanoparticle-mediated cancer immunotherapy and effective synergy with PD-L1 blockade.

title: Continuous biosignal acquisition beyond the limit of epidermal turnover
abstract: Advances in chronic wearable biosignal acquisition are reviewed, emphasizing biointerfaces, wireless power, multimodal sensing, analytics, and challenges in epidermal turnover, accuracy, power and clinical translation.

title: Polar selenide single crystal LiGa 0.5 In 0.5 Se 2 with a bulk photovoltaic effect for highly sensitive self-driven X-ray detection
abstract: This study reveals the intrinsic polarization-induced bulk photovoltaic effect in LiGa 0.5 In 0.5 Se 2 crystals, enabling fabricated self-driven X-ray detectors with high sensitivity (354 μC Gy −1 cm −2 ) and low detection limit (125.5 nGy s −1 ).

title: Nanozyme-enabled cancer theranostics: bridging enzyme mimicry and material intelligence
abstract: Nanozymes, an innovative class of biocatalytic nanomaterials, have emerged as transformative tools in cancer theranostics.

title: Recent advances in atomic layer deposition of superconducting thin films: a review
abstract: ALD of superconducting films for various high performance applications.

title: Integrating magnetic Co-nanopillars in a NbN-based VAN thin film as a multifunctional hybrid metamaterial
abstract: An epitaxial NbN–Co VAN thin film was deposited on a MgO substrate with a cubic NbN phase, which presents ferromagnet properties with strong out-of-plane magnetic anisotropy. This hybrid metamaterial could find future applications in device design.

title: Real-time autonomous control of a continuous macroscopic process as demonstrated by plastic forming
abstract: To meet the need for more adaptable and expedient approaches in research and manufacturing, we present a continuous autonomous system that leverages real-time, in situ characterization and an active-learning-based decision-making processor.

title: Photothermal and robust supramolecular soft material crosslinked via dinuclear heterodentate coordination
abstract: Efficient, green, and intrinsic solar-photothermal conversion elastomers are crucial for sustainable energy solutions.

title: Coexistence of altermagnetism and robust ferroelectricity in a bulk MnO wurtzite structure
abstract: Altermagnetism is a new class of material with zero net magnetization, but having a nonrelativistic spin-split band structure.

title: Artificial intelligence guided search for van der Waals materials with high optical anisotropy
abstract: By integrating artificial intelligence into the material search process, we gain new insights into the correlation between crystallographic parameters and optical anisotropy of van der Waals (vdW) materials, which was confirmed experimentally.

title: Dual relaxation behaviors driven by a homogeneous and stable dual-interface charge layer based on an EGaIn absorber
abstract: Interface engineering, by modulating defect distribution and impedance at interfaces and inducing interfacial polarization, has proven to be an effective strategy for optimizing dielectric properties.

title: Harnessing mesoporous g-C 3 N 4 -based photocatalytic materials for sustainable fuel production via solar energy conversion: a review
abstract: This TOC signifies the mp-g-CN photocatalyst enriched with a finely tuned functional porous surface and enhanced optoelectronic features, which suitably enables solar fuel production by using renewable stuffs to maintain the circular energy cycle.

title: Thermosensitive liposomal nanomedicine-functionalized photothermal composite scaffolds for light-guided cancer therapy
abstract: An implantable ALA@lipo/Au/Gel/PGA composite scaffold functionalized with ALA-encapsulated thermosensitive liposomes enables photothermal therapy and photodynamic therapy for synergistic anticancer effects.

title: A Schiff-base-modified Cu nanocluster with redox dual-catalytic sites and fluorescence sensing for the degradation and detection of atrazine
abstract: Simultaneous detection and degradation of Atrazine, a widely used and heavily contaminating pesticide, using Cu nanocluster-based catalyst.

title: A porous network of boron-doped IrO 2 nanoneedles with enhanced mass activity for acidic oxygen evolution reactions
abstract: Activity, stability, and cost are essential aspects in exploring high-performance IrO 2 -based OER electrocatalysts. This work strikes a balance among these aspects via B doping-driven 1D anisotropic growth of a porous network of IrO 2 nanoneedles.

title: Ab initio simulation of molecular crystal regrowth of paracetamol from solution
abstract: This work successfully utilises the DFT/ESM-RISM model to elucidate the mechanisms underpinning the novel ‘self-healing’ regrowth behaviour of crystalline paracetamol post breakage.

title: Correction: Application of carbon-based nanomaterials in Alzheimer's disease
abstract: Correction for ‘Application of carbon-based nanomaterials in Alzheimer's disease’ by Mengyao Bai et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2024, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4MH01256A.

title: Cardiac troponin detection in one drop of saliva or urine for 10-minute assessment of acute myocardial infarction
abstract: The printed immunosensing photonic crystal biochip (PCB) is the first non-invasive tech for 10-min CTnT detection (0.25 pg/mL, 10 μL), outperforming ELISA with ≥99.8% consistency for AMI rapid diagnosis and health management.

title: Environmentally responsive semi-interpenetrating network microcapsules with enhanced stability for corrosion protection
abstract: Robust pH-responsive microcapsules with high encapsulation efficiency and controlled release profiles were prepared via complexation–diffusion assembly, offering a viable platform for corrosion mitigation in CO 2 -intensive acidic environments.

title: A critical overview of the influence of graphene and its analogues on nematic liquid crystals
abstract: This review highlights advances in integrating 2D materials with nematic liquid crystals, focusing on structure–property relationships, molecular alignment strategies, polymer networks, lyotropics, applications, and key challenges and opportunities.

title: Memristive neuromorphic interfaces: integrating sensory modalities with artificial neural networks
abstract: This review explores recent advances in mimicking biological sensory systems—receptors, neurons, and synapses—using memristors. It also outlines methods to attain the necessary device traits and implement systems effectively.

title: High strength, self-activating ability and fast adhesion of polyurethane adhesives based on rosin structure in different environments
abstract: The polyol was synthesized using dehydroabietic acid (DA) to prepare the adhesive DAP–PU. And the hydrophobic effect of the hydrogenated phenanthrene ring of the DA was utilized to exclude interfacial water and achieve underwater adhesion.

title: Rigid-flexible coupling modular mechanical metamaterials with tunable elasto-plastic properties
abstract: Rigid-flexible modular metamaterials enable tunable stiffness at component and hierarchical levels, with self-locking dynamic protection. Interchangeable parts and customizable properties offer portable, low-cost solutions for rapid deployment.

title: Inducing superior electrical performances in textured CaBi 2 Ta 2 O 9 based ceramics
abstract: Combining crystallographic phenomenological theory and first-principles calculations reveals that [100]-textured CBTa ceramics achieve a 68% d 33 enhancement. This grain plastic rearrangement also improves resistivity without reducing T C .

title: Differential intracellular influence of cancer cells and normal cells on magnetothermal properties and magnetic hyperthermal effects of magnetic nanoparticles
abstract: Differential influence of the intracellular microenvironment of cancer cells and normal cells on the magnetothermal properties of internalized MNPs and the magnetic hyperthermia efficiency.

title: An asymmetrically tipped MoS 2 /CdS heterostructure for high-performance photocatalytic plastic reforming
abstract: A 1D MoS 2 /CdS heterojunction photocatalyst enables plastic waste photoreforming, yielding 97.7 mmol g −1 h −1 H 2 without cocatalysts. Interfacial asymmetry enhances charge separation, driving PLA oxidation to lactate and pyruvate.

title: Tailoring surface structures in Mn-based Prussian blue analogues for enhanced NH 4 + transport and high-performance aqueous batteries
abstract: An innovative and facile method is employed for the surface modification of Mn–PBA, which is beneficial for the rapid diffusion of NH 4 + and cycling stability in aqueous ammonium-ion storage.

title: From optoelectronics to scintillation applications: the versatility of lead-free halide double perovskites
abstract: Lead-free halide double perovskites feature low toxicity and structural tunability, making them promising candidates for sustainable optoelectronic and scintillation applications.

title: Progress and perspectives of rapid Joule heating for the preparation of highly efficient catalysts
abstract: Functional catalytic materials play an important role in environmental, biological, energy, and other fields, wherein unique properties can be endowed through various synthesis strategies.

title: Innovative engineering strategies and mechanistic insights for enhanced carbon-based electrocatalysts in sustainable H 2 O 2 production
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive overview of the latest key advancements in materials engineering strategies for carbon-based electrocatalysts for H 2 O 2 production.

title: Functionalization of monolithic MOF thin films with hydrocarbon chains to achieve superhydrophobic surfaces with tunable water adhesion strength
abstract: Grafting hydrophobic hydrocarbon chains to the outer surface of a MOF thin film with well-defined spacing induces entropy-driven superhydrophobic performance.
